1# -*- coding: utf-8 -*- 

2"""Location: ./mcpgateway/transports/streamablehttp_transport.py 

3Copyright 2026 

4SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 

5Authors: Keval Mahajan 

6 

7Streamable HTTP Transport Implementation. 

8This module implements Streamable Http transport for MCP 

9 

10Key components include: 

11- SessionManagerWrapper: Manages the lifecycle of streamable HTTP sessions 

12- Configuration options for: 

13 1. stateful/stateless operation 

14 2. JSON response mode or SSE streams 

15- InMemoryEventStore: A simple in-memory event storage system for maintaining session state 

16 

17Examples: 

18 >>> # Test module imports 

19 >>> from mcpgateway.transports.streamablehttp_transport import ( 

20 ... EventEntry, StreamBuffer, InMemoryEventStore, SessionManagerWrapper 

21 ... ) 

22 >>> 

23 >>> # Verify classes are available 

24 >>> EventEntry.__name__ 

25 'EventEntry' 

26 >>> StreamBuffer.__name__ 

27 'StreamBuffer' 

28 >>> InMemoryEventStore.__name__ 

29 'InMemoryEventStore' 

30 >>> SessionManagerWrapper.__name__ 

31 'SessionManagerWrapper' 

32""" 

33

108# Initialize logging service first 

109logging_service = LoggingService() 

110logger = logging_service.get_logger(__name__) 

111 

112 

113def _maybe_open_initialize_span(body: bytes, *, mcp_session_id: Optional[str], server_id: Optional[str]) -> Optional[ContextManager[Any]]: 

114 """Return an active span context manager for raw MCP initialize traffic. 

115 

116 Args: 

117 body: Raw JSON-RPC request body bytes. 

118 mcp_session_id: Session identifier from the request headers when present. 

119 server_id: Effective virtual server identifier for the request, if any. 

120 

121 Returns: 

122 Active span context manager for initialize requests, otherwise a no-op context. 

123 """ 

124 try: 

125 payload = orjson.loads(body) 

126 except orjson.JSONDecodeError: 

127 return None 

128 

129 if not isinstance(payload, dict) or str(payload.get("method") or "").strip() != "initialize": 

130 return None 

131 

132 params = payload.get("params") 

133 if not isinstance(params, dict): 

134 params = {} 

135 

136 session_id = params.get("sessionId") or params.get("session_id") 

137 if not session_id and mcp_session_id and mcp_session_id != "not-provided": 

138 session_id = mcp_session_id 

139 

140 span_attributes: Dict[str, Any] = { 

141 "mcp.protocol_version": params.get("protocolVersion") or params.get("protocol_version"), 

142 "mcp.session_id": session_id, 

143 "server.id": server_id, 

144 } 

145 return create_span("mcp.initialize", span_attributes) 

146 

147 

148def _normalize_mcp_prompt_arguments(arguments: Any) -> Optional[List[types.PromptArgument]]: 

149 """Convert internal prompt-argument objects to MCP prompt arguments. 

150 

151 The prompt service returns internal schema models, while the MCP transport 

152 must emit ``mcp.types.PromptArgument`` instances. Pydantic does not treat 

153 different model classes as interchangeable, so raw pass-through raises 

154 validation errors during prompt listing. 

155 

156 Args: 

157 arguments: Prompt arguments from internal services. Items may already be 

158 ``mcp.types.PromptArgument`` instances, dicts, or other Pydantic 

159 models with matching attributes. 

160 

161 Returns: 

162 Normalized MCP prompt arguments, or ``None`` when the prompt has no 

163 argument list. 

164 """ 

165 if arguments is None: 

166 return None 

167 

168 normalized_arguments: List[types.PromptArgument] = [] 

169 for argument in arguments: 

170 if isinstance(argument, types.PromptArgument): 

171 normalized_arguments.append(argument) 

172 else: 

173 normalized_arguments.append(types.PromptArgument.model_validate(argument, from_attributes=True)) 

174 return normalized_arguments 

175 

176 

177def _safe_str_attr(obj: Any, attr: str) -> Optional[str]: 

178 """Extract an attribute as ``str | None``, guarding against non-string values. 

179 

180 Args: 

181 obj: The object to read the attribute from. 

182 attr: The attribute name to extract. 

183 

184 Returns: 

185 The attribute value if it is a ``str``, otherwise ``None``. 

186 """ 

187 value = getattr(obj, attr, None) 

188 return value if isinstance(value, str) else None 

189 

190 

191def _to_mcp_prompt(prompt: Any) -> types.Prompt: 

192 """Convert an internal prompt object to the MCP transport model. 

193 

194 Args: 

195 prompt: Internal prompt object returned by prompt_service. 

196 

197 Returns: 

198 MCP prompt model suitable for protocol responses. 

199 """ 

200 title = _safe_str_attr(prompt, "title") 

201 

202 meta = getattr(prompt, "meta", None) 

203 if not isinstance(meta, dict): 

204 meta = None 

205 

206 return types.Prompt(name=prompt.name, title=title, description=prompt.description, arguments=_normalize_mcp_prompt_arguments(getattr(prompt, "arguments", None)), meta=meta) 

207 

208 

209def _record_mcp_auth_cache_event(outcome: str) -> None: 

210 """Best-effort Prometheus counter update for MCP auth cache flow. 

211 

212 Args: 

213 outcome: Cache-flow outcome label to emit. 

214 """ 

215 try: 

216 mcp_auth_cache_events_counter.labels(outcome=outcome).inc() 

217 except Exception: 

218 pass # nosec B110 - Metrics must not break auth flow 

219 

220 

221# Precompiled regex for server ID extraction from path. 

222# SECURITY: Uses [^/]+ (any non-slash characters) instead of a restrictive hex-only 

223# class to ensure ALL server-scoped paths are captured. A narrow regex caused non-hex 

224# IDs (e.g. "xyz") to silently fall through to unscoped global behaviour (#3891). 

225_SERVER_ID_RE: Pattern[str] = re.compile(r"^/servers/(?P<server_id>[^/]+)/mcp") 

226 

227# Pattern that detects a server-scoped MCP path even when _SERVER_ID_RE doesn't 

228# match (e.g. empty segment: /servers//mcp). Used as a defense-in-depth guard. 

229_SERVER_SCOPED_PATH_RE: Pattern[str] = re.compile(r"^/servers/.*/mcp(?:/)?$") 

230 

231# Sentinel returned by _validate_server_id to signal that an error response 

232# has already been sent and the caller should return immediately. 

233_REJECT = object() 

234 

235 

236# ASGI scope key for propagating gateway context from middleware to MCP handlers 

237_MCPGATEWAY_CONTEXT_KEY = "_mcpgateway_context" 

238 

239# Initialize ToolService, PromptService, ResourceService, CompletionService and MCP Server 

240tool_service: ToolService = ToolService() 

241prompt_service: PromptService = PromptService() 

242resource_service: ResourceService = ResourceService() 

243completion_service: CompletionService = CompletionService() 

244 

245mcp_app: Server[Any] = Server("mcp-streamable-http") 

246 

247server_id_var: contextvars.ContextVar[str] = contextvars.ContextVar("server_id", default="default_server_id") 

248# First-Party 

249# request_headers_var + user_context_var live in `mcpgateway.transports.context` 

250# so service-layer code can read them without importing this module (which 

251# would create an import cycle via prompt/tool/resource services). Imported 

252# here for backwards-compat: external callers that already do 

253# `from mcpgateway.transports.streamablehttp_transport import request_headers_var` 

254# keep working. 

255from mcpgateway.transports.context import request_headers_var, user_context_var, user_identity_var # noqa: E402 # pylint: disable=wrong-import-position 

256 

257_oauth_checked_var: contextvars.ContextVar[bool] = contextvars.ContextVar("_oauth_checked", default=False) 

258 

259 

260class OAuthAuthResult(Enum): 

261 """Outcome of an OAuth access-token verification attempt. 

262 

263 Used by :meth:`_StreamableHttpAuthHandler._try_oauth_access_token` to 

264 communicate whether the token was handled, rejected, or not applicable 

265 without relying on a tri-state ``Optional[bool]``. 

266 """ 

267 

268 SUCCESS = "success" # Verified; user context populated. 

269 FAILED = "failed" # Verification attempted and rejected; error already sent. 

270 NOT_APPLICABLE = "not_applicable" # Target server does not use OAuth; caller should continue. 

271 

272 

273def _resolve_authorization_servers(oauth_config: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[str]: 

274 """Normalise a virtual-server ``oauth_config`` into an issuer allowlist. 

275 

276 Accepts either the plural ``authorization_servers`` (list of URLs) or the 

277 legacy singular ``authorization_server`` (single URL string). Returns an 

278 empty list when neither key is present or both are empty. 

279 

280 Args: 

281 oauth_config: The ``server.oauth_config`` dict from a virtual server. 

282 

283 Returns: 

284 A list of allowed issuer URLs, with surrounding whitespace stripped. 

285 """ 

286 servers = oauth_config.get("authorization_servers") or [] 

287 if isinstance(servers, list): 

288 cleaned = [s.strip() for s in servers if isinstance(s, str) and s.strip()] 

289 if cleaned: 

290 non_https = [s for s in cleaned if not s.lower().startswith("https://")] 

291 if non_https: 

292 logger.warning("Ignoring non-HTTPS authorization_servers (SSRF risk): %s", non_https) 

293 cleaned = [s for s in cleaned if s.lower().startswith("https://")] 

294 return cleaned 

295 singular = oauth_config.get("authorization_server") 

296 if isinstance(singular, str) and singular.strip(): 

297 url = singular.strip() 

298 if not url.lower().startswith("https://"): 

299 logger.warning("Ignoring non-HTTPS authorization_server (SSRF risk): %s", url) 

300 return [] 

301 return [url] 

302 return [] 

303 

304 

305_shared_session_registry: Optional[Any] = None 

306_rust_event_store_client: Optional[httpx.AsyncClient] = None 

307_rust_event_store_client_lock = asyncio.Lock() 

308_RUST_EVENT_STORE_DEFAULT_KEY_PREFIX = "mcpgw:eventstore" 

309 

310# ------------------------------ Event store ------------------------------ 

311 

312 

313@dataclass 

314class EventEntry: 

315 """ 

316 Represents an event entry in the event store. 

317 

318 Examples: 

319 >>> # Create an event entry 

320 >>> from mcp.types import JSONRPCMessage 

321 >>> message = JSONRPCMessage(jsonrpc="2.0", method="test", id=1) 

322 >>> entry = EventEntry(event_id="test-123", stream_id="stream-456", message=message, seq_num=0) 

323 >>> entry.event_id 

324 'test-123' 

325 >>> entry.stream_id 

326 'stream-456' 

327 >>> entry.seq_num 

328 0 

329 >>> # Access message attributes through model_dump() for Pydantic v2 

330 >>> message_dict = message.model_dump() 

331 >>> message_dict['jsonrpc'] 

332 '2.0' 

333 >>> message_dict['method'] 

334 'test' 

335 >>> message_dict['id'] 

336 1 

337 """ 

338 

339 event_id: EventId 

340 stream_id: StreamId 

341 message: JSONRPCMessage 

342 seq_num: int 

343 

344 

345@dataclass 

346class StreamBuffer: 

347 """ 

348 Ring buffer for per-stream event storage with O(1) position lookup. 

349 

350 Tracks sequence numbers to enable efficient replay without scanning. 

351 Events are stored at position (seq_num % capacity) in the entries list. 

352 

353 Examples: 

354 >>> # Create a stream buffer with capacity 3 

355 >>> buffer = StreamBuffer(entries=[None, None, None]) 

356 >>> buffer.start_seq 

357 0 

358 >>> buffer.next_seq 

359 0 

360 >>> buffer.count 

361 0 

362 >>> len(buffer) 

363 0 

364 

365 >>> # Simulate adding an entry 

366 >>> buffer.next_seq = 1 

367 >>> buffer.count = 1 

368 >>> len(buffer) 

369 1 

370 """ 

371 

372 entries: list[EventEntry | None] 

373 start_seq: int = 0 # oldest seq still buffered 

374 next_seq: int = 0 # seq assigned to next insert 

375 count: int = 0 

376 

377 def __len__(self) -> int: 

378 """Return the number of events currently in the buffer. 

379 

380 Returns: 

381 int: The count of events in the buffer. 

382 """ 

383 return self.count 

384 

385 

386class InMemoryEventStore(EventStore): 

387 """ 

388 Simple in-memory implementation of the EventStore interface for resumability. 

389 This is primarily intended for examples and testing, not for production use 

390 where a persistent storage solution would be more appropriate. 

391 

392 This implementation keeps only the last N events per stream for memory efficiency. 

393 Uses a ring buffer with per-stream sequence numbers for O(1) event lookup and O(k) replay. 

394 

395 Examples: 

396 >>> # Create event store with default max events 

397 >>> store = InMemoryEventStore() 

398 >>> store.max_events_per_stream 

399 100 

400 >>> len(store.streams) 

401 0 

402 >>> len(store.event_index) 

403 0 

404 

405 >>> # Create event store with custom max events 

406 >>> store = InMemoryEventStore(max_events_per_stream=50) 

407 >>> store.max_events_per_stream 

408 50 

409 

410 >>> # Test event store initialization 

411 >>> store = InMemoryEventStore() 

412 >>> hasattr(store, 'streams') 

413 True 

414 >>> hasattr(store, 'event_index') 

415 True 

416 >>> isinstance(store.streams, dict) 

417 True 

418 >>> isinstance(store.event_index, dict) 

419 True 

420 """ 

421 

422 def __init__(self, max_events_per_stream: int = 100): 

423 """Initialize the event store. 

424 

425 Args: 

426 max_events_per_stream: Maximum number of events to keep per stream 

427 

428 Examples: 

429 >>> # Test initialization with default value 

430 >>> store = InMemoryEventStore() 

431 >>> store.max_events_per_stream 

432 100 

433 >>> store.streams == {} 

434 True 

435 >>> store.event_index == {} 

436 True 

437 

438 >>> # Test initialization with custom value 

439 >>> store = InMemoryEventStore(max_events_per_stream=25) 

440 >>> store.max_events_per_stream 

441 25 

442 """ 

443 self.max_events_per_stream = max_events_per_stream 

444 # Per-stream ring buffers for O(1) position lookup 

445 self.streams: dict[StreamId, StreamBuffer] = {} 

446 # event_id -> EventEntry for quick lookup 

447 self.event_index: dict[EventId, EventEntry] = {} 

448 

449 async def store_event(self, stream_id: StreamId, message: JSONRPCMessage) -> EventId: 

450 """ 

451 Stores an event with a generated event ID. 

452 

453 Args: 

454 stream_id (StreamId): The ID of the stream. 

455 message (JSONRPCMessage): The message to store. 

456 

457 Returns: 

458 EventId: The ID of the stored event. 

459 

460 Examples: 

461 >>> # Test storing an event 

462 >>> import asyncio 

463 >>> from mcp.types import JSONRPCMessage 

464 >>> store = InMemoryEventStore(max_events_per_stream=5) 

465 >>> message = JSONRPCMessage(jsonrpc="2.0", method="test", id=1) 

466 >>> event_id = asyncio.run(store.store_event("stream-1", message)) 

467 >>> isinstance(event_id, str) 

468 True 

469 >>> len(event_id) > 0 

470 True 

471 >>> len(store.streams) 

472 1 

473 >>> len(store.event_index) 

474 1 

475 >>> "stream-1" in store.streams 

476 True 

477 >>> event_id in store.event_index 

478 True 

479 

480 >>> # Test storing multiple events in same stream 

481 >>> message2 = JSONRPCMessage(jsonrpc="2.0", method="test2", id=2) 

482 >>> event_id2 = asyncio.run(store.store_event("stream-1", message2)) 

483 >>> len(store.streams["stream-1"]) 

484 2 

485 >>> len(store.event_index) 

486 2 

487 

488 >>> # Test ring buffer overflow 

489 >>> store2 = InMemoryEventStore(max_events_per_stream=2) 

490 >>> msg1 = JSONRPCMessage(jsonrpc="2.0", method="m1", id=1) 

491 >>> msg2 = JSONRPCMessage(jsonrpc="2.0", method="m2", id=2) 

492 >>> msg3 = JSONRPCMessage(jsonrpc="2.0", method="m3", id=3) 

493 >>> id1 = asyncio.run(store2.store_event("stream-2", msg1)) 

494 >>> id2 = asyncio.run(store2.store_event("stream-2", msg2)) 

495 >>> # Now buffer is full, adding third will remove first 

496 >>> id3 = asyncio.run(store2.store_event("stream-2", msg3)) 

497 >>> len(store2.streams["stream-2"]) 

498 2 

499 >>> id1 in store2.event_index # First event removed 

500 False 

501 >>> id2 in store2.event_index and id3 in store2.event_index 

502 True 

503 """ 

504 # Get or create ring buffer for this stream 

505 buffer = self.streams.get(stream_id) 

506 if buffer is None: 

507 buffer = StreamBuffer(entries=[None] * self.max_events_per_stream) 

508 self.streams[stream_id] = buffer 

509 

510 # Assign per-stream sequence number 

511 seq_num = buffer.next_seq 

512 buffer.next_seq += 1 

513 idx = seq_num % self.max_events_per_stream 

514 

515 # Handle eviction if buffer is full 

516 if buffer.count == self.max_events_per_stream: 

517 evicted = buffer.entries[idx] 

518 if evicted is not None: 

519 self.event_index.pop(evicted.event_id, None) 

520 buffer.start_seq += 1 

521 else: 

522 if buffer.count == 0: 

523 buffer.start_seq = seq_num 

524 buffer.count += 1 

525 

526 # Create and store the new event entry 

527 event_id = str(uuid4()) 

528 event_entry = EventEntry(event_id=event_id, stream_id=stream_id, message=message, seq_num=seq_num) 

529 buffer.entries[idx] = event_entry 

530 self.event_index[event_id] = event_entry 

531 

532 return event_id 

533 

534 async def replay_events_after( 

535 self, 

536 last_event_id: EventId, 

537 send_callback: EventCallback, 

538 ) -> Union[StreamId, None]: 

539 """ 

540 Replays events that occurred after the specified event ID. 

541 

542 Uses O(1) lookup via event_index and O(k) replay where k is the number 

543 of events to replay, avoiding the previous O(n) full scan. 

544 

545 Args: 

546 last_event_id (EventId): The ID of the last received event. Replay starts after this event. 

547 send_callback (EventCallback): Async callback to send each replayed event. 

548 

549 Returns: 

550 StreamId | None: The stream ID if the event is found and replayed, otherwise None. 

551 

552 Examples: 

553 >>> # Test replaying events 

554 >>> import asyncio 

555 >>> from mcp.types import JSONRPCMessage 

556 >>> store = InMemoryEventStore() 

557 >>> message1 = JSONRPCMessage(jsonrpc="2.0", method="test1", id=1) 

558 >>> message2 = JSONRPCMessage(jsonrpc="2.0", method="test2", id=2) 

559 >>> message3 = JSONRPCMessage(jsonrpc="2.0", method="test3", id=3) 

560 >>> 

561 >>> # Store events 

562 >>> event_id1 = asyncio.run(store.store_event("stream-1", message1)) 

563 >>> event_id2 = asyncio.run(store.store_event("stream-1", message2)) 

564 >>> event_id3 = asyncio.run(store.store_event("stream-1", message3)) 

565 >>> 

566 >>> # Test replay after first event 

567 >>> replayed_events = [] 

568 >>> async def mock_callback(event_message): 

569 ... replayed_events.append(event_message) 

570 >>> 

571 >>> result = asyncio.run(store.replay_events_after(event_id1, mock_callback)) 

572 >>> result 

573 'stream-1' 

574 >>> len(replayed_events) 

575 2 

576 

577 >>> # Test replay with non-existent event 

578 >>> result = asyncio.run(store.replay_events_after("non-existent", mock_callback)) 

579 >>> result is None 

580 True 

581 """ 

582 # O(1) lookup in event_index 

583 last_event = self.event_index.get(last_event_id) 

584 if last_event is None: 

585 logger.warning("Event ID %s not found in store", last_event_id) 

586 return None 

587 

588 buffer = self.streams.get(last_event.stream_id) 

589 if buffer is None: 

590 return None 

591 

592 # Validate that the event's seq_num is still within the buffer range 

593 if last_event.seq_num < buffer.start_seq or last_event.seq_num >= buffer.next_seq: 

594 return None 

595 

596 # O(k) replay: iterate from last_event.seq_num + 1 to buffer.next_seq - 1 

597 for seq in range(last_event.seq_num + 1, buffer.next_seq): 

598 entry = buffer.entries[seq % self.max_events_per_stream] 

599 # Guard: skip if slot is empty or has been overwritten by a different seq 

600 if entry is None or entry.seq_num != seq: 

601 continue 

602 await send_callback(EventMessage(entry.message, entry.event_id)) 

603 

604 return last_event.stream_id 

605 

606 

607class RustEventStore(EventStore): 

608 """Rust-backed event store that delegates resumable stream state to the sidecar.""" 

609 

610 def __init__(self, max_events_per_stream: int = 100, ttl: int = 3600, key_prefix: str = _RUST_EVENT_STORE_DEFAULT_KEY_PREFIX): 

611 """Initialize the Rust-backed event store wrapper. 

612 

613 Args: 

614 max_events_per_stream: Maximum number of events retained per stream. 

615 ttl: Event retention time in seconds. 

616 key_prefix: Redis key prefix shared with the Rust sidecar. 

617 """ 

618 self.max_events_per_stream = max_events_per_stream 

619 self.ttl = ttl 

620 self.key_prefix = key_prefix.rstrip(":") 

621 

622 async def store_event(self, stream_id: StreamId, message: JSONRPCMessage | None) -> EventId: 

623 """Store an event in the Rust-backed resumable event store. 

624 

625 Args: 

626 stream_id: Stream that owns the event. 

627 message: JSON-RPC payload to persist for replay. 

628 

629 Returns: 

630 The generated event identifier returned by the Rust sidecar. 

631 

632 Raises: 

633 RuntimeError: If the Rust sidecar event store is unavailable or returns invalid data. 

634 """ 

635 client = await _get_rust_event_store_client() 

636 message_dict = None if message is None else (message.model_dump() if hasattr(message, "model_dump") else dict(message)) 

637 response = await client.post( 

638 _build_rust_runtime_internal_url("/_internal/event-store/store"), 

639 json={ 

640 "streamId": stream_id, 

641 "message": message_dict, 

642 "keyPrefix": self.key_prefix, 

643 "maxEventsPerStream": self.max_events_per_stream, 

644 "ttlSeconds": self.ttl, 

645 }, 

646 timeout=httpx.Timeout(settings.experimental_rust_mcp_runtime_timeout_seconds), 

647 follow_redirects=False, 

648 ) 

649 response.raise_for_status() 

650 payload = response.json() 

651 event_id = payload.get("eventId") 

652 if not isinstance(event_id, str) or not event_id: 

653 raise RuntimeError("Rust event store returned an invalid eventId") 

654 return event_id 

655 

656 async def replay_events_after(self, last_event_id: EventId, send_callback: EventCallback) -> Union[StreamId, None]: 

657 """Replay events newer than ``last_event_id`` through the provided callback. 

658 

659 Args: 

660 last_event_id: Last event acknowledged by the reconnecting client. 

661 send_callback: Callback invoked for each replayed event payload. 

662 

663 Returns: 

664 The associated stream identifier when replay succeeds, else ``None``. 

665 """ 

666 client = await _get_rust_event_store_client() 

667 response = await client.post( 

668 _build_rust_runtime_internal_url("/_internal/event-store/replay"), 

669 json={ 

670 "lastEventId": last_event_id, 

671 "keyPrefix": self.key_prefix, 

672 }, 

673 timeout=httpx.Timeout(settings.experimental_rust_mcp_runtime_timeout_seconds), 

674 follow_redirects=False, 

675 ) 

676 response.raise_for_status() 

677 payload = response.json() 

678 stream_id = payload.get("streamId") 

679 if not isinstance(stream_id, str) or not stream_id: 

680 return None 

681 for event in payload.get("events", []): 

682 if not isinstance(event, dict): 

683 continue 

684 await send_callback(event.get("message")) 

685 return stream_id 

686 

687 

688async def _get_rust_event_store_client() -> httpx.AsyncClient: 

689 """Return the HTTP client used for Python -> Rust event-store calls. 

690 

691 Returns: 

692 An async HTTP client configured for Rust event-store access. 

693 """ 

694 global _rust_event_store_client # pylint: disable=global-statement 

695 

696 uds_path = settings.experimental_rust_mcp_runtime_uds 

697 if not uds_path: 

698 return await get_http_client() 

699 

700 if _rust_event_store_client is not None: 

701 return _rust_event_store_client 

702 

703 async with _rust_event_store_client_lock: 

704 if _rust_event_store_client is None: 

705 _rust_event_store_client = httpx.AsyncClient( 

706 transport=httpx.AsyncHTTPTransport(uds=uds_path), 

707 limits=get_http_limits(), 

708 timeout=httpx.Timeout(settings.experimental_rust_mcp_runtime_timeout_seconds), 

709 follow_redirects=False, 

710 ) 

711 return _rust_event_store_client 

712 

713 

714def _build_rust_runtime_internal_url(path: str) -> str: 

715 """Build a Rust sidecar internal URL for UDS or loopback HTTP transport. 

716 

717 Args: 

718 path: Internal Rust runtime path to append to the configured base URL. 

719 

720 Returns: 

721 Absolute URL targeting the Rust sidecar over HTTP or UDS-backed transport. 

722 """ 

723 base = urlsplit(settings.experimental_rust_mcp_runtime_url) 

724 base_path = base.path.rstrip("/") 

725 target_path = f"{base_path}{path}" if base_path else path 

726 return urlunsplit((base.scheme, base.netloc, target_path, "", "")) 

727 

728 

729# ------------------------------ Streamable HTTP Transport ------------------------------ 

730 

731 

732@asynccontextmanager 

733async def get_db() -> AsyncGenerator[Session, Any]: 

734 """ 

735 Asynchronous context manager for database sessions. 

736 

737 Commits the transaction on successful completion to avoid implicit rollbacks 

738 for read-only operations. Rolls back explicitly on exception. Handles 

739 asyncio.CancelledError explicitly to prevent transaction leaks when MCP 

740 handlers are cancelled (client disconnect, timeout, etc.). 

741 

742 Yields: 

743 A database session instance from SessionLocal. 

744 Ensures the session is closed after use. 

745 

746 Raises: 

747 asyncio.CancelledError: Re-raised after rollback and close on task cancellation. 

748 Exception: Re-raises any exception after rolling back the transaction. 

749 

750 Examples: 

751 >>> # Test database context manager 

752 >>> import asyncio 

753 >>> async def test_db(): 

754 ... async with get_db() as db: 

755 ... return db is not None 

756 >>> result = asyncio.run(test_db()) 

757 >>> result 

758 True 

759 """ 

760 db = SessionLocal() 

761 try: 

762 yield db 

763 db.commit() 

764 except asyncio.CancelledError: 

765 # Handle cancellation explicitly to prevent transaction leaks. 

766 # When MCP handlers are cancelled (client disconnect, timeout, etc.), 

767 # we must rollback and close the session before re-raising. 

768 try: 

769 db.rollback() 

770 except Exception: 

771 pass # nosec B110 - Best effort rollback on cancellation 

772 try: 

773 db.close() 

774 except Exception: 

775 pass # nosec B110 - Best effort close on cancellation 

776 raise 

777 except Exception: 

778 try: 

779 db.rollback() 

780 except Exception: 

781 try: 

782 db.invalidate() 

783 except Exception: 

784 pass # nosec B110 - Best effort cleanup on connection failure 

785 raise 

786 finally: 

787 db.close() 

788 

789 

790def get_user_email_from_context() -> str: 

791 """Extract user email from the current user context. 

792 

793 Returns: 

794 User email address or 'unknown' if not available 

795 """ 

796 user = user_context_var.get() 

797 if isinstance(user, dict): 

798 # First try 'email', then 'sub' (JWT standard claim) 

799 return user.get("email") or user.get("sub") or "unknown" 

800 return str(user) if user else "unknown" 

801 

802 

803def _should_enforce_streamable_rbac(user_context: Optional[dict[str, Any]]) -> bool: 

804 """Return True when request originated from authenticated Streamable HTTP middleware. 

805 

806 Direct unit tests may call MCP handlers without middleware context; those 

807 invocations should preserve historical behavior and avoid forced RBAC checks. 

808 

809 Args: 

810 user_context: Request user context propagated by Streamable HTTP auth middleware. 

811 

812 Returns: 

813 bool: ``True`` when permission checks should be enforced for this request. 

814 """ 

815 return isinstance(user_context, dict) and user_context.get("is_authenticated", False) is True 

816 

817 

818def _build_public_base_url(scope: Scope) -> str: 

819 """Derive the public-facing base URL (``scheme://host[/root_path]``) from an ASGI scope. 

820 

821 Inspects ``x-forwarded-proto`` and ``host`` headers first (reverse-proxy 

822 scenario), then falls back to ``scope["scheme"]`` and ``scope["server"]``. 

823 Includes ``scope["root_path"]`` so that deployments behind a reverse proxy 

824 with a path prefix emit the correct public URL. 

825 

826 Args: 

827 scope: ASGI connection scope. 

828 

829 Returns: 

830 Base URL with trailing root_path (no trailing slash), or ``""`` if 

831 construction fails. 

832 """ 

833 try: 

834 headers = Headers(scope=scope) 

835 forwarded_proto = headers.get("x-forwarded-proto") 

836 if forwarded_proto: 

837 proto = forwarded_proto.split(",")[0].strip().lower() 

838 else: 

839 proto = scope.get("scheme", "https") 

840 if proto not in ("http", "https"): 

841 proto = "https" 

842 

843 host = headers.get("host") 

844 if not host: 

845 server_tuple = scope.get("server") 

846 if server_tuple: 

847 host_addr, port = server_tuple 

848 # Wrap IPv6 addresses in brackets per RFC 2732 

849 if ":" in str(host_addr): 

850 host_addr = f"[{host_addr}]" 

851 default_port = 443 if proto == "https" else 80 

852 host = f"{host_addr}:{port}" if port != default_port else host_addr 

853 else: 

854 return "" 

855 

856 root_path = (scope.get("root_path") or settings.app_root_path or "").rstrip("/") 

857 return f"{proto}://{host}{root_path}" 

858 except (AttributeError, KeyError, TypeError, ValueError) as exc: 

859 # Malformed ASGI scope (missing keys, wrong types, unparseable 

860 # header bytes). Log at WARNING so upstream 401s have a forensic 

861 # trail — callers treat "" as a fail-closed sentinel and reject 

862 # the request, but without this log operators have no visibility 

863 # into *why* URL derivation failed. Include the request path and 

864 # client tuple so a flood of failures can be traced to a specific 

865 # route or peer. 

866 scope_path = scope.get("path") if isinstance(scope, dict) else None 

867 scope_client = scope.get("client") if isinstance(scope, dict) else None 

868 logger.warning( 

869 "Failed to derive public base URL from ASGI scope (path=%s, client=%s): %s: %s", 

870 scope_path, 

871 scope_client, 

872 type(exc).__name__, 

873 exc, 

874 ) 

875 return "" 

876 

877 

878def _build_server_resource_url(scope: Scope, server_id: str) -> str: 

879 """Construct the canonical MCP resource URL for a virtual server. 

880 

881 This is the URL that RFC 8707 / RFC 9728 identify as the *resource* — 

882 i.e. the value clients should request tokens for and that the server 

883 MUST enforce as the access-token ``aud`` claim. 

884 

885 .. important:: 

886 The base URL is derived from :data:`settings.app_domain`, **not** the 

887 inbound ``Host`` / ``X-Forwarded-Host`` headers. Both are 

888 caller-controlled (any client can send an arbitrary ``Host``; a 

889 permissive proxy can forward one too), so trusting them here would 

890 let a client bypass audience binding simply by sending the hostname 

891 their token happens to name. ``settings.app_domain`` is operator-set 

892 at deployment time and therefore a safe trust anchor. Operators MUST 

893 set it to the gateway's public URL for OAuth audience validation to 

894 be effective. 

895 

896 Args: 

897 scope: ASGI connection scope (retained for signature compatibility 

898 with related helpers; unused in the resource-URL derivation). 

899 server_id: Virtual-server identifier. 

900 

901 Returns: 

902 Fully-qualified resource URL string, or ``""`` if construction fails. 

903 """ 

904 del scope # intentionally ignored — see docstring 

905 try: 

906 raw = str(settings.app_domain).rstrip("/") 

907 except (AttributeError, ValueError) as exc: 

908 logger.warning("settings.app_domain is not a usable URL: %s: %s", type(exc).__name__, exc) 

909 return "" 

910 if not raw: 

911 return "" 

912 return f"{raw}/servers/{server_id}/mcp" 

913 

914 

915def _build_resource_metadata_url(scope: Scope, server_id: str) -> str: 

916 """Construct the RFC 9728 OAuth Protected Resource Metadata URL from ASGI scope. 

917 

918 Args: 

919 scope: ASGI connection scope. 

920 server_id: Virtual-server identifier. 

921 

922 Returns: 

923 Fully-qualified URL string, or ``""`` if construction fails. 

924 """ 

925 base = _build_public_base_url(scope) 

926 if not base: 

927 return "" 

928 return f"{base}/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/servers/{server_id}/mcp" 

929 

930 

931def _is_valid_audience(value: Any) -> bool: 

932 """Return True if ``value`` is an RFC 7519-compliant ``aud`` claim. 

933 

934 Per RFC 7519 §4.1.3 the ``aud`` claim is either a single ``StringOrURI`` 

935 or an array of them. PyJWT only enforces this shape when ``verify_aud`` 

936 is enabled, so a misconfigured IdP could otherwise mint tokens with 

937 ``aud`` values like ``{"foo": "bar"}`` or ``42``. Persisting such a value 

938 as the server's ``resource`` would then cause a ``TypeError`` inside 

939 PyJWT on the *next* request (when it is forwarded as ``audience=...``), 

940 locking the server's auth path until the operator manually clears the 

941 bogus value. Validate up-front and skip persist on malformed shapes. 

942 

943 Args: 

944 value: The raw ``aud`` claim value to validate. 

945 

946 Returns: 

947 True iff ``value`` is a non-empty string or a non-empty list of 

948 non-empty strings; False otherwise. 

949 """ 

950 if isinstance(value, str): 

951 return bool(value.strip()) 

952 if isinstance(value, list): 

953 return bool(value) and all(isinstance(item, str) and item.strip() for item in value) 

954 return False 

955 

956 

957def _persist_learned_server_audience(server_id: str, verified_claims: dict[str, Any], db: Session) -> None: 

958 """Persist the ``aud`` claim from a verified OAuth token as ``resource``. 

959 

960 Called after successful signature + issuer verification of an inbound MCP 

961 request. The token's ``aud`` claim is trustworthy at this point and 

962 represents the IdP's authoritative audience value. 

963 

964 Persistence is **first-write-only**: the learned audience is written only 

965 when ``oauth_config["resource"]`` is currently falsy. The MCP request path 

966 only enforces server-level access on the inbound caller — it does not 

967 require ``servers.update``. Allowing every authenticated request to 

968 overwrite shared server configuration would let any user with server 

969 access mutate global state on behalf of all other users (last-user-wins). 

970 To re-learn a stale audience after an IdP change, an admin must clear the 

971 ``resource`` field via the server update API (which does enforce 

972 ``servers.update``). This also avoids two related failure modes: 

973 

974 * Silently collapsing an operator-configured multi-audience list 

975 (e.g. ``["aud-a", "aud-b"]``) down to whichever single ``aud`` a given 

976 token happened to carry, breaking other clients. 

977 * Silently changing the operator's audience binding when an IdP starts 

978 emitting unexpected ``aud`` values; an explicit auth failure is 

979 preferable so the operator notices. 

980 

981 Empty strings and empty lists count as unset (Python truthiness), so an 

982 admin can clear the field to either falsy value to trigger re-learning 

983 on the next request. 

984 

985 Malformed ``aud`` values (anything other than a non-empty string or a 

986 non-empty list of non-empty strings) are rejected up-front via 

987 :func:`_is_valid_audience` so a bogus persist cannot break subsequent 

988 requests inside PyJWT. 

989 

990 This is a best-effort operation: opaque tokens, missing ``aud`` claims, 

991 and already-set (truthy) resources are silently skipped; downstream DB 

992 failures are logged but do not affect the current request's authentication 

993 outcome. 

994 

995 Args: 

996 server_id: Virtual-server identifier. 

997 verified_claims: Decoded and *signature-verified* JWT claims. 

998 db: Active database session. 

999 """ 

1000 raw_aud = verified_claims.get("aud") 

1001 if not _is_valid_audience(raw_aud): 

1002 if raw_aud is not None: 

1003 logger.warning( 

1004 "Refusing to persist malformed aud claim for server %s (type=%s)", 

1005 sanitize_for_log(server_id), 

1006 type(raw_aud).__name__, 

1007 ) 

1008 return 

1009 

1010 try: 

1011 server = db.execute(select(DbServer).where(DbServer.id == server_id)).scalar_one_or_none() 

1012 if server is None or not server.oauth_config: 

1013 return 

1014 

1015 # First-write-only: do not overwrite an existing usable resource. 

1016 # Empty strings and empty lists are treated as unset (Python 

1017 # truthiness) so an admin can clear the field via the server update 

1018 # API to trigger re-learning. See docstring for the authorization 

1019 # rationale. 

1020 if server.oauth_config.get("resource"): 

1021 return 

1022 

1023 updated_config = dict(server.oauth_config) 

1024 updated_config["resource"] = raw_aud 

1025 server.oauth_config = updated_config 

1026 db.flush() 

1027 logger.info( 

1028 "Learned OAuth audience from IdP token for server %s; persisted as resource", 

1029 sanitize_for_log(server_id), 

1030 ) 

1031 except Exception: 

1032 logger.warning("Failed to persist learned audience for server %s", server_id, exc_info=True) 

1033 

1034 

1035async def _check_server_oauth_enforcement(server_id: str, user_context: Optional[dict[str, Any]]) -> None: 

1036 """Reject unauthenticated callers when a server requires OAuth. 

1037 

1038 Looks up the server's ``oauth_enabled`` flag and raises 

1039 ``OAuthRequiredError`` when the flag is set but the caller is not 

1040 authenticated. This closes the gap where OAuth capability is 

1041 *advertised* (via RFC 9728 ``experimental.oauth``) but never 

1042 *enforced* on subsequent MCP requests. 

1043 

1044 The result is cached in ``_oauth_checked_var`` for the lifetime of 

1045 the request so that handler-level defense-in-depth calls do not 

1046 repeat the DB query already performed by the middleware. 

1047 

1048 .. note:: 

1049 SSE transport is not covered here because it already requires 

1050 authentication unconditionally. 

1051 

1052 Args: 

1053 server_id: Virtual-server identifier extracted from the URL path. 

1054 user_context: User context set by ``streamable_http_auth`` middleware. 

1055 

1056 Raises: 

1057 OAuthRequiredError: When the server requires OAuth and the caller has 

1058 not provided valid authentication credentials. 

1059 OAuthEnforcementUnavailableError: When the database or session is 

1060 unavailable and the server's ``oauth_enabled`` flag cannot be 

1061 verified (fail-closed). 

1062 """ 

1063 if _oauth_checked_var.get(False): 

1064 return # Already checked during this request 

1065 

1066 if not server_id or server_id == "default_server_id": 

1067 return # No server context — nothing to enforce 

1068 

1069 is_authenticated = (user_context or {}).get("is_authenticated", False) 

1070 if is_authenticated: 

1071 _oauth_checked_var.set(True) 

1072 return # Already authenticated — no need to check 

1073 

1074 try: 

1075 async with get_db() as db: 

1076 server = db.execute(select(DbServer).where(DbServer.id == server_id)).scalar_one_or_none() 

1077 if server and server.oauth_enabled: 

1078 logger.warning("OAuth required for server %s but caller is unauthenticated", server_id) 

1079 raise OAuthRequiredError( 

1080 "This server requires OAuth authentication. Please provide a valid access token.", 

1081 server_id=server_id, 

1082 ) 

1083 _oauth_checked_var.set(True) 

1084 except SQLAlchemyError as exc: 

1085 # DB lookup failure — fail-closed for security. 

1086 logger.error("OAuth enforcement DB lookup failed for server %s: %s", server_id, exc) 

1087 raise OAuthEnforcementUnavailableError( 

1088 f"Unable to verify OAuth requirements for server {server_id}", 

1089 server_id=server_id, 

1090 ) from exc 

1091 

1092 

1093async def _check_streamable_permission( 

1094 *, 

1095 user_context: dict[str, Any], 

1096 permission: str, 

1097 allow_admin_bypass: bool = True, 

1098 check_any_team: bool = False, 

1099) -> bool: 

1100 """Evaluate RBAC permission for a Streamable HTTP request context. 

1101 

1102 Args: 

1103 user_context: Authenticated user context from Streamable HTTP middleware. 

1104 permission: Permission name to evaluate (for example ``tools.execute``). 

1105 allow_admin_bypass: Whether unrestricted admin tokens can bypass team checks. 

1106 check_any_team: Whether any matching team grants permission. 

1107 

1108 Returns: 

1109 bool: ``True`` when the caller is authorized for ``permission``. 

1110 """ 

1111 user_email = user_context.get("email") 

1112 if not user_email: 

1113 return False 

1114 

1115 try: 

1116 async with get_db() as db: 

1117 permission_service = PermissionService(db) 

1118 granted = await permission_service.check_permission( 

1119 user_email=user_email, 

1120 permission=permission, 

1121 token_teams=user_context.get("teams"), 

1122 allow_admin_bypass=allow_admin_bypass, 

1123 check_any_team=check_any_team, 

1124 ) 

1125 if not granted: 

1126 logger.warning("Streamable HTTP RBAC denied: user=%s, permission=%s", user_email, permission) 

1127 return granted 

1128 except Exception as exc: 

1129 logger.warning("Streamable HTTP RBAC check failed for %s / %s: %s", user_email, permission, exc) 

1130 return False 

1131 

1132 

1133def _check_scoped_permission(user_context: dict[str, Any], permission: str) -> bool: 

1134 """Check if token scoped permissions allow this operation. 

1135 

1136 Args: 

1137 user_context: User context dict (may contain 'scoped_permissions' key). 

1138 permission: Permission to check. 

1139 

1140 Returns: 

1141 True if allowed (no scope cap, wildcard, or permission present). 

1142 """ 

1143 scoped = user_context.get("scoped_permissions") 

1144 if not scoped: # None or empty list = defer to RBAC 

1145 return True 

1146 if "*" in scoped: 

1147 return True 

1148 allowed = permission in scoped 

1149 if not allowed: 

1150 logger.warning("Streamable HTTP token scope denied: user=%s, required=%s", user_context.get("email"), permission) 

1151 return allowed 

1152 

1153 

1154def _check_any_team_for_server_scoped_rbac(user_context: dict[str, Any] | None, server_id: str | None) -> bool: 

1155 """Return whether Streamable HTTP RBAC should check across team-scoped roles. 

1156 

1157 Server-scoped MCP routes (``/servers/<id>/mcp``) should authorize team-bound 

1158 callers against the specific virtual server context. Session tokens already do 

1159 this via ``check_any_team=True`` because they have no single explicit team_id. 

1160 Team-scoped API tokens need the same treatment on server-scoped routes; otherwise 

1161 they are evaluated only in global scope and incorrectly denied. 

1162 

1163 Args: 

1164 user_context: Current authenticated MCP user context, if any. 

1165 server_id: Effective virtual server identifier for the MCP request. 

1166 

1167 Returns: 

1168 ``True`` when RBAC should search across the caller's token teams. 

1169 """ 

1170 if not user_context: 

1171 return False 

1172 if user_context.get("token_use") == "session": 

1173 return True 

1174 return bool(server_id) and bool(user_context.get("teams")) 

1175 

1176 

1177def set_shared_session_registry(session_registry: Any) -> None: 

1178 """Set the process-wide session registry used by Streamable HTTP helpers. 

1179 

1180 Args: 

1181 session_registry: Registry instance created by application bootstrap. 

1182 """ 

1183 global _shared_session_registry # pylint: disable=global-statement 

1184 _shared_session_registry = session_registry 

1185 

1186 

1187def _get_shared_session_registry() -> Optional[Any]: 

1188 """Return the process-wide session registry reference. 

1189 

1190 Returns: 

1191 Optional[Any]: Session registry instance, or ``None`` when unavailable. 

1192 """ 

1193 return _shared_session_registry 

1194 

1195 

1196async def _claim_streamable_session_owner(session_id: str, owner_email: str) -> Optional[str]: 

1197 """Claim or resolve the logical owner for a Streamable HTTP session. 

1198 

1199 Args: 

1200 session_id: Logical MCP session identifier to claim. 

1201 owner_email: Caller email that should own the session. 

1202 

1203 Returns: 

1204 Optional[str]: Effective owner email after claim, or ``None`` if unavailable. 

1205 """ 

1206 if not session_id or not owner_email: 

1207 return None 

1208 

1209 session_registry = _get_shared_session_registry() 

1210 if session_registry is None: 

1211 return None 

1212 

1213 try: 

1214 return await session_registry.claim_session_owner(session_id, owner_email) 

1215 except Exception as exc: 

1216 logger.warning("Failed to claim session owner for %s: %s", session_id, exc) 

1217 return None 

1218 

1219 

1220async def _validate_streamable_session_access( 

1221 *, 

1222 mcp_session_id: Optional[str], 

1223 user_context: Optional[dict[str, Any]], 

1224 rpc_method: Optional[str] = None, 

1225) -> tuple[bool, int, str]: 

1226 """Authorize access to a stateful Streamable HTTP session identifier. 

1227 

1228 Args: 

1229 mcp_session_id: Session identifier from request headers. 

1230 user_context: Authenticated user context for the current request. 

1231 rpc_method: JSON-RPC method name when available. 

1232 

1233 Returns: 

1234 Tuple ``(allowed, deny_status_code, deny_message)``. 

1235 """ 

1236 if not settings.use_stateful_sessions: 

1237 return True, 200, "" 

1238 

1239 if not mcp_session_id or mcp_session_id == "not-provided": 

1240 return True, 200, "" 

1241 

1242 if not _should_enforce_streamable_rbac(user_context): 

1243 return True, 200, "" 

1244 

1245 if isinstance(user_context, dict) and user_context.get("_rust_session_validated") is True: 

1246 return True, 200, "" 

1247 

1248 # Initialize establishes a new session and is authorized separately. 

1249 if (rpc_method or "").strip() == "initialize": 

1250 return True, 200, "" 

1251 

1252 requester_email = user_context.get("email") if isinstance(user_context, dict) else None 

1253 requester_is_admin = bool(user_context.get("is_admin", False)) if isinstance(user_context, dict) else False 

1254 

1255 session_registry = _get_shared_session_registry() 

1256 if session_registry is None: 

1257 return False, HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN, "Session ownership unavailable" 

1258 

1259 try: 

1260 session_owner = await session_registry.get_session_owner(mcp_session_id) 

1261 except Exception as exc: 

1262 logger.warning("Failed to get session owner for %s: %s", mcp_session_id, exc) 

1263 return False, HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN, "Session ownership unavailable" 

1264 

1265 if session_owner: 

1266 if requester_is_admin: 

1267 return True, 200, "" 

1268 if requester_email and requester_email == session_owner: 

1269 return True, 200, "" 

1270 return False, HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN, "Session access denied" 

1271 

1272 try: 

1273 session_exists = await session_registry.session_exists(mcp_session_id) 

1274 except Exception as exc: 

1275 logger.warning("Failed to check session existence for %s: %s", mcp_session_id, exc) 

1276 return False, HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN, "Session ownership unavailable" 

1277 

1278 if session_exists is False: 

1279 return False, HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, "Session not found" 

1280 return False, HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN, "Session owner metadata unavailable" 

1281 

1282 

1283def _build_paginated_params(meta: Optional[Any]) -> Optional[PaginatedRequestParams]: 

1284 """Build a ``PaginatedRequestParams`` carrying ``_meta`` when provided. 

1285 

1286 Args: 

1287 meta: Request metadata (_meta) from the original MCP request, or ``None``. 

1288 

1289 Returns: 

1290 A ``PaginatedRequestParams`` instance with ``_meta`` set, or ``None`` when *meta* is falsy. 

1291 """ 

1292 if not meta: 

1293 return None 

1294 # CWE-532: log only key names, never values which may carry PII/tokens 

1295 logger.debug("Forwarding _meta to remote gateway (keys: %s)", sorted(meta.keys()) if isinstance(meta, dict) else type(meta).__name__) 

1296 return PaginatedRequestParams(_meta=meta) 

1297 

1298 

1299async def _send_streamable_http_json_response(send: Send, *, status_code: int, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None: 

1300 """Send a JSON response for Streamable HTTP request handling paths. 

1301 

1302 Args: 

1303 send: ASGI send callable. 

1304 status_code: HTTP status code for the response. 

1305 payload: JSON-serializable response payload. 

1306 """ 

1307 body = orjson.dumps(payload) 

1308 await send( 

1309 { 

1310 "type": "http.response.start", 

1311 "status": status_code, 

1312 "headers": [(b"content-type", b"application/json"), (b"content-length", str(len(body)).encode())], 

1313 } 

1314 ) 

1315 await send({"type": "http.response.body", "body": body}) 

1316 

1317 

1318async def _close_streamable_http_session( 

1319 *, 

1320 mcp_session_id: str, 

1321 user_context: Optional[dict[str, Any]], 

1322) -> tuple[int, dict[str, Any]]: 

1323 """Close a stateful Streamable HTTP session deterministically. 

1324 

1325 Args: 

1326 mcp_session_id: Stateful MCP session identifier to close. 

1327 user_context: Authenticated requester context used for ownership checks. 

1328 

1329 Returns: 

1330 Tuple ``(status_code, payload)``. 

1331 """ 

1332 session_allowed, deny_status, deny_detail = await _validate_streamable_session_access( 

1333 mcp_session_id=mcp_session_id, 

1334 user_context=user_context, 

1335 rpc_method=None, 

1336 ) 

1337 if not session_allowed: 

1338 return deny_status, {"detail": deny_detail} 

1339 

1340 session_registry = _get_shared_session_registry() 

1341 if session_registry is None: 

1342 return HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN, {"detail": "Session ownership unavailable"} 

1343 

1344 try: 

1345 await session_registry.remove_session(mcp_session_id) 

1346 except Exception as exc: 

1347 logger.warning(f"Failed to remove streamable session {mcp_session_id}: {exc}") 

1348 return HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, {"detail": "Failed to close session"} 

1349 

1350 # Best-effort cleanup for multi-worker session-affinity ownership records. 

1351 try: 

1352 # First-Party 

1353 from mcpgateway.services.session_affinity import get_session_affinity # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1354 

1355 await get_session_affinity().cleanup_session_owner(mcp_session_id) 

1356 except RuntimeError: 

1357 pass 

1358 except Exception as exc: 

1359 logger.debug(f"Failed to clear affinity owner for session {mcp_session_id}: {exc}") 

1360 

1361 return HTTP_200_OK, {"jsonrpc": "2.0", "result": {}} 

1362 

1363 

1364async def _proxy_list_tools_to_gateway(gateway: Any, request_headers: dict, user_context: dict, meta: Optional[Any] = None) -> List[types.Tool]: # pylint: disable=unused-argument 

1365 """Proxy tools/list request directly to remote MCP gateway using MCP SDK. 

1366 

1367 Args: 

1368 gateway: Gateway ORM instance 

1369 request_headers: Request headers from client 

1370 user_context: User context (not used - _meta comes from MCP SDK) 

1371 meta: Request metadata (_meta) from the original request 

1372 

1373 Returns: 

1374 List of Tool objects from remote server 

1375 """ 

1376 try: 

1377 # Prepare headers with gateway auth 

1378 headers = build_gateway_auth_headers(gateway) 

1379 

1380 # Forward passthrough headers using shared utility (includes X-Upstream-Authorization rename) 

1381 if request_headers: 

1382 gw_passthrough = gateway.passthrough_headers if hasattr(gateway, "passthrough_headers") and gateway.passthrough_headers is not None else None 

1383 if gw_passthrough is not None: 

1384 passthrough_allowed = gw_passthrough 

1385 else: 

1386 with SessionLocal() as db: 

1387 passthrough_allowed = global_config_cache.get_passthrough_headers(db, settings.default_passthrough_headers) 

1388 headers = compute_passthrough_headers_cached( 

1389 request_headers, 

1390 headers, 

1391 passthrough_allowed, 

1392 gateway_auth_type=gateway.auth_type if hasattr(gateway, "auth_type") else None, 

1393 gateway_passthrough_headers=gw_passthrough, 

1394 ) 

1395 

1396 # Inject identity propagation headers 

1397 identity = user_identity_var.get() 

1398 if identity: 

1399 headers.update(build_identity_headers(identity, gateway)) 

1400 

1401 # Use MCP SDK to connect and list tools 

1402 async with streamablehttp_client(url=gateway.url, headers=headers, timeout=settings.mcpgateway_direct_proxy_timeout) as (read_stream, write_stream, _get_session_id): 

1403 async with ClientSession(read_stream, write_stream) as session: 

1404 await session.initialize() 

1405 

1406 # List tools with _meta forwarded 

1407 result = await session.list_tools(params=_build_paginated_params(meta)) 

1408 return result.tools 

1409 

1410 except Exception as e: 

1411 logger.exception("Error proxying tools/list to gateway %s: %s", gateway.id, e) 

1412 return [] 

1413 

1414 

1415async def _proxy_list_resources_to_gateway(gateway: Any, request_headers: dict, user_context: dict, meta: Optional[Any] = None) -> List[types.Resource]: # pylint: disable=unused-argument 

1416 """Proxy resources/list request directly to remote MCP gateway using MCP SDK. 

1417 

1418 Args: 

1419 gateway: Gateway ORM instance 

1420 request_headers: Request headers from client 

1421 user_context: User context (not used - _meta comes from MCP SDK) 

1422 meta: Request metadata (_meta) from the original request 

1423 

1424 Returns: 

1425 List of Resource objects from remote server 

1426 """ 

1427 try: 

1428 # Prepare headers with gateway auth 

1429 headers = build_gateway_auth_headers(gateway) 

1430 

1431 # Forward passthrough headers using shared utility (includes X-Upstream-Authorization rename) 

1432 if request_headers: 

1433 gw_passthrough = gateway.passthrough_headers if hasattr(gateway, "passthrough_headers") and gateway.passthrough_headers is not None else None 

1434 if gw_passthrough is not None: 

1435 passthrough_allowed = gw_passthrough 

1436 else: 

1437 with SessionLocal() as db: 

1438 passthrough_allowed = global_config_cache.get_passthrough_headers(db, settings.default_passthrough_headers) 

1439 headers = compute_passthrough_headers_cached( 

1440 request_headers, 

1441 headers, 

1442 passthrough_allowed, 

1443 gateway_auth_type=gateway.auth_type if hasattr(gateway, "auth_type") else None, 

1444 gateway_passthrough_headers=gw_passthrough, 

1445 ) 

1446 

1447 # Inject identity propagation headers 

1448 identity = user_identity_var.get() 

1449 if identity: 

1450 headers.update(build_identity_headers(identity, gateway)) 

1451 

1452 logger.info("Proxying resources/list to gateway %s at %s", gateway.id, gateway.url) 

1453 if meta: 

1454 # CWE-532: log only key names, never values which may carry PII/tokens 

1455 logger.debug("Forwarding _meta to remote gateway (keys: %s)", sorted(meta.keys()) if isinstance(meta, dict) else type(meta).__name__) 

1456 

1457 # Use MCP SDK to connect and list resources 

1458 async with streamablehttp_client(url=gateway.url, headers=headers, timeout=settings.mcpgateway_direct_proxy_timeout) as (read_stream, write_stream, _get_session_id): 

1459 async with ClientSession(read_stream, write_stream) as session: 

1460 await session.initialize() 

1461 

1462 # List resources with _meta forwarded 

1463 result = await session.list_resources(params=_build_paginated_params(meta)) 

1464 

1465 logger.info("Received %s resources from gateway %s", len(result.resources), gateway.id) 

1466 return result.resources 

1467 

1468 except Exception as e: 

1469 logger.exception("Error proxying resources/list to gateway %s: %s", gateway.id, e) 

1470 return [] 

1471 

1472 

1473async def _proxy_read_resource_to_gateway(gateway: Any, resource_uri: str, user_context: dict, meta: Optional[Any] = None) -> List[Any]: # pylint: disable=unused-argument 

1474 """Proxy resources/read request directly to remote MCP gateway using MCP SDK. 

1475 

1476 Args: 

1477 gateway: Gateway ORM instance 

1478 resource_uri: URI of the resource to read 

1479 user_context: User context (not used - auth comes from gateway config) 

1480 meta: Request metadata (_meta) from the original request 

1481 

1482 Returns: 

1483 List of content objects (TextResourceContents or BlobResourceContents) from remote server 

1484 """ 

1485 try: 

1486 # Prepare headers with gateway auth 

1487 headers = build_gateway_auth_headers(gateway) 

1488 

1489 # Get request headers 

1490 request_headers = request_headers_var.get() 

1491 

1492 # Forward X-Context-Forge-Gateway-Id header 

1493 gw_id = extract_gateway_id_from_headers(request_headers) 

1494 if gw_id: 

1495 headers[GATEWAY_ID_HEADER] = gw_id 

1496 

1497 # Forward passthrough headers using shared utility (includes X-Upstream-Authorization rename) 

1498 if request_headers: 

1499 gw_passthrough = gateway.passthrough_headers if hasattr(gateway, "passthrough_headers") and gateway.passthrough_headers is not None else None 

1500 if gw_passthrough is not None: 

1501 passthrough_allowed = gw_passthrough 

1502 else: 

1503 with SessionLocal() as db: 

1504 passthrough_allowed = global_config_cache.get_passthrough_headers(db, settings.default_passthrough_headers) 

1505 headers = compute_passthrough_headers_cached( 

1506 request_headers, 

1507 headers, 

1508 passthrough_allowed, 

1509 gateway_auth_type=gateway.auth_type if hasattr(gateway, "auth_type") else None, 

1510 gateway_passthrough_headers=gw_passthrough, 

1511 ) 

1512 

1513 # Inject identity propagation headers 

1514 identity = user_identity_var.get() 

1515 if identity: 

1516 headers.update(build_identity_headers(identity, gateway)) 

1517 

1518 logger.info("Proxying resources/read for %s to gateway %s at %s", resource_uri, gateway.id, gateway.url) 

1519 if meta: 

1520 # CWE-532: log only key names, never values which may carry PII/tokens 

1521 logger.debug("Forwarding _meta to remote gateway (keys: %s)", sorted(meta.keys()) if isinstance(meta, dict) else type(meta).__name__) 

1522 

1523 # Use MCP SDK to connect and read resource 

1524 async with streamablehttp_client(url=gateway.url, headers=headers, timeout=settings.mcpgateway_direct_proxy_timeout) as (read_stream, write_stream, _get_session_id): 

1525 async with ClientSession(read_stream, write_stream) as session: 

1526 await session.initialize() 

1527 

1528 # Prepare request params with _meta if provided 

1529 if meta: 

1530 # Create params and inject _meta 

1531 # by_alias=True ensures the alias "_meta" key is written so 

1532 # model_validate resolves it correctly (fixes CWE-20 silent drop) 

1533 request_params = ReadResourceRequestParams(uri=resource_uri) 

1534 request_params_dict = request_params.model_dump(by_alias=True) 

1535 request_params_dict["_meta"] = meta 

1536 

1537 # Send request with _meta 

1538 result = await session.send_request( 

1539 types.ClientRequest(ReadResourceRequest(params=ReadResourceRequestParams.model_validate(request_params_dict))), 

1540 types.ReadResourceResult, 

1541 ) 

1542 else: 

1543 # No _meta, use simple read_resource 

1544 result = await session.read_resource(uri=resource_uri) 

1545 

1546 logger.info("Received %s content items from gateway %s for resource %s", len(result.contents), gateway.id, resource_uri) 

1547 return result.contents 

1548 

1549 except Exception as e: 

1550 logger.exception("Error proxying resources/read to gateway %s for resource %s: %s", gateway.id, resource_uri, e) 

1551 return [] 

1552 

1553 

1554def _truthy_is_error(result: Any) -> bool: 

1555 """Return ``True`` when ``result`` represents an MCP error response. 

1556 

1557 Centralises the #4202 egress check so both the local and pooled 

1558 branches of :func:`call_tool` read ``is_error`` identically, and so 

1559 the mitigation for MagicMock-attribute pollution in the test suite 

1560 lives in one place. Semantics: 

1561 

1562 - A real ``mcpgateway.common.models.ToolResult`` has ``is_error`` as a 

1563 typed ``bool`` with default ``False``. MCP SDK ``CallToolResult`` 

1564 instances expose the same flag as camelCase ``isError``. Both must 

1565 be recognised so error responses with declared output schemas bypass 

1566 server-side structured-output validation. 

1567 - A ``unittest.mock.MagicMock`` auto-materialises attributes as truthy 

1568 ``MagicMock`` objects. ``bool(mock.is_error)`` reports ``True`` 

1569 even when the test author didn't explicitly set the attribute, 

1570 which used to silently route success-path transport tests into the 

1571 ``CallToolResult`` short-circuit branch. The ``is True`` identity 

1572 check rejects that shape. 

1573 - The identity check does silently coerce truthy non-``True`` values 

1574 (e.g. ``1``, ``"true"``) to ``False``. That's acceptable because 

1575 production ``ToolResult.is_error`` is typed ``bool`` and the 

1576 failure mode of a misbehaving upstream producing a truthy non-bool 

1577 is already caught at the ``_coerce_to_tool_result`` boundary. 

1578 

1579 Args: 

1580 result: Upstream tool result (ToolResult, MCP SDK CallToolResult, 

1581 MagicMock, or any duck-typed carrier). 

1582 

1583 Returns: 

1584 ``True`` only when ``result.is_error`` or ``result.isError`` is 

1585 literally ``True``. 

1586 """ 

1587 return getattr(result, "is_error", False) is True or getattr(result, "isError", False) is True 

1588 

1589 

1590@mcp_app.call_tool(validate_input=False) 

1591async def call_tool(name: str, arguments: dict) -> Union[ 

1592 types.CallToolResult, 

1593 List[Union[types.TextContent, types.ImageContent, types.AudioContent, types.ResourceLink, types.EmbeddedResource]], 

1594 Tuple[List[Union[types.TextContent, types.ImageContent, types.AudioContent, types.ResourceLink, types.EmbeddedResource]], Dict[str, Any]], 

1595]: 

1596 """ 

1597 Handles tool invocation via the MCP Server. 

1598 

1599 Note: validate_input=False disables the MCP SDK's built-in JSON Schema validation. 

1600 This is necessary because the SDK uses jsonschema.validate() which internally calls 

1601 check_schema() with the default validator. Schemas using older draft features 

1602 (e.g., Draft 4 style exclusiveMinimum: true) fail this validation. The gateway 

1603 handles schema validation separately in tool_service.py with multi-draft support. 

1604 

1605 This function supports the MCP protocol's tool calling with structured content validation. 

1606 In direct_proxy mode, returns the raw CallToolResult from the remote server. 

1607 In normal mode, converts ToolResult to CallToolResult with content normalization. 

1608 

1609 Args: 

1610 name (str): The name of the tool to invoke. 

1611 arguments (dict): A dictionary of arguments to pass to the tool. 

1612 

1613 Returns: 

1614 types.CallToolResult: MCP SDK CallToolResult with content and optional structuredContent. 

1615 

1616 Raises: 

1617 PermissionError: If the caller lacks ``tools.execute`` permission. 

1618 Exception: Re-raised after logging to allow MCP SDK to convert to JSON-RPC error response. 

1619 

1620 Examples: 

1621 >>> # Test call_tool function signature 

1622 >>> import inspect 

1623 >>> sig = inspect.signature(call_tool) 

1624 >>> list(sig.parameters.keys()) 

1625 ['name', 'arguments'] 

1626 >>> sig.parameters['name'].annotation 

1627 <class 'str'> 

1628 >>> sig.parameters['arguments'].annotation 

1629 <class 'dict'> 

1630 """ 

1631 server_id, request_headers, user_context = await _get_request_context_or_default() 

1632 

1633 meta_data = None 

1634 # Extract _meta from request context if available 

1635 try: 

1636 ctx = mcp_app.request_context 

1637 if ctx and ctx.meta is not None: 

1638 meta_data = ctx.meta.model_dump() 

1639 except LookupError: 

1640 # request_context might not be active in some edge cases (e.g. tests) 

1641 logger.debug("No active request context found") 

1642 

1643 # First-Party 

1644 from mcpgateway.auth_context import get_scoped_visibility_from_user_context # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1645 

1646 # Extract Layer-1 visibility filter from user context 

1647 user_email, token_teams = get_scoped_visibility_from_user_context(user_context) 

1648 

1649 # Enforce per-server OAuth requirement in permissive mode (defense-in-depth). 

1650 # When mcp_require_auth=True, the middleware already guarantees authentication. 

1651 # Note: OAuthEnforcementUnavailableError is intentionally uncaught here — 

1652 # the middleware (streamable_http_auth) catches it and returns 503. If the 

1653 # middleware is somehow bypassed, an uncaught 500 is acceptable and will be 

1654 # logged by the ASGI server. 

1655 if not settings.mcp_require_auth: 

1656 await _check_server_oauth_enforcement(server_id, user_context) 

1657 

1658 if _should_enforce_streamable_rbac(user_context): 

1659 # Layer 1: Token scope cap 

1660 if not _check_scoped_permission(user_context, "tools.execute"): 

1661 raise PermissionError(_ACCESS_DENIED_MSG) 

1662 # Layer 2: RBAC check 

1663 # Session tokens have no explicit team_id; check across all team-scoped roles. 

1664 # Mirrors the @require_permission decorator's check_any_team fallback (rbac.py:562-576). 

1665 has_execute_permission = await _check_streamable_permission( 

1666 user_context=user_context, 

1667 permission="tools.execute", 

1668 check_any_team=_check_any_team_for_server_scoped_rbac(user_context, server_id), 

1669 ) 

1670 if not has_execute_permission: 

1671 raise PermissionError(_ACCESS_DENIED_MSG) 

1672 

1673 # Check if we're in direct_proxy mode by looking for X-Context-Forge-Gateway-Id header 

1674 gateway_id_from_header = extract_gateway_id_from_headers(request_headers) 

1675 

1676 # If X-Context-Forge-Gateway-Id header is present, use direct proxy mode 

1677 if gateway_id_from_header: 

1678 try: # Check if this gateway is in direct_proxy mode 

1679 async with get_db() as check_db: 

1680 gateway = check_db.execute(select(DbGateway).where(DbGateway.id == gateway_id_from_header)).scalar_one_or_none() 

1681 if gateway and getattr(gateway, "gateway_mode", "cache") == "direct_proxy" and settings.mcpgateway_direct_proxy_enabled: 

1682 # SECURITY: Check gateway access before allowing direct proxy 

1683 if not await check_gateway_access(check_db, gateway, user_email, token_teams): 

1684 logger.warning("Access denied to gateway %s in direct_proxy mode for user %s", gateway_id_from_header, user_email) 

1685 return types.CallToolResult(content=[types.TextContent(type="text", text=f"Tool not found: {name}")], isError=True) 

1686 

1687 logger.info("Using direct_proxy mode for tool '%s' via gateway %s", name, gateway_id_from_header) 

1688 

1689 # Use direct proxy method - returns raw CallToolResult from remote server 

1690 # Return it directly without any normalization 

1691 return await tool_service.invoke_tool_direct( 

1692 gateway_id=gateway_id_from_header, 

1693 name=name, 

1694 arguments=arguments, 

1695 request_headers=request_headers, 

1696 meta_data=meta_data, 

1697 user_email=user_email, 

1698 token_teams=token_teams, 

1699 user_context=user_identity_var.get(), 

1700 ) 

1701 except Exception as e: 

1702 logger.error("Direct proxy mode failed for gateway %s: %s", gateway_id_from_header, e) 

1703 return types.CallToolResult(content=[types.TextContent(type="text", text="Direct proxy tool invocation failed")], isError=True) 

1704 

1705 # Normal mode: use standard tool invocation with normalization 

1706 # Use the already-recovered user_context (works for both ContextVar and stateful session paths) 

1707 app_user_email = (user_context.get("email") or user_context.get("sub") or "unknown") if user_context else "unknown" 

1708 

1709 # Multi-worker session affinity: check if we should forward to another worker 

1710 # Check both x-mcp-session-id (internal/forwarded) and mcp-session-id (client protocol header) 

1711 mcp_session_id = None 

1712 if request_headers: 

1713 request_headers_lower = {k.lower(): v for k, v in request_headers.items()} 

1714 mcp_session_id = request_headers_lower.get("x-mcp-session-id") or request_headers_lower.get("mcp-session-id") 

1715 if settings.mcpgateway_session_affinity_enabled and mcp_session_id: 

1716 try: 

1717 # First-Party 

1718 from mcpgateway.cache.tool_lookup_cache import tool_lookup_cache # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1719 from mcpgateway.services.session_affinity import get_session_affinity # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1720 from mcpgateway.services.session_affinity import SessionAffinity # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1721 

1722 if not SessionAffinity.is_valid_mcp_session_id(mcp_session_id): 

1723 logger.debug("Invalid MCP session id for Streamable HTTP tool affinity, executing locally") 

1724 raise RuntimeError("invalid mcp session id") 

1725 

1726 pool = get_session_affinity() 

1727 

1728 # Register session mapping BEFORE checking forwarding (same pattern as SSE) 

1729 # This ensures ownership is registered atomically so forward_request_to_owner() works 

1730 try: 

1731 cached = await tool_lookup_cache.get(name) 

1732 if cached and cached.get("status") == "active": 

1733 gateway_info = cached.get("gateway") 

1734 if gateway_info: 

1735 url = gateway_info.get("url") 

1736 gateway_id = gateway_info.get("id", "") 

1737 transport_type = gateway_info.get("transport", "streamablehttp") 

1738 if url: 

1739 await pool.register_session_mapping(mcp_session_id, url, gateway_id, transport_type, user_email) 

1740 except Exception as e: 

1741 logger.error("Failed to pre-register session mapping for Streamable HTTP: %s", e) 

1742 

1743 forwarded_response = await pool.forward_request_to_owner( 

1744 mcp_session_id, 

1745 {"method": "tools/call", "params": {"name": name, "arguments": arguments, "_meta": meta_data}, "headers": dict(request_headers) if request_headers else {}}, 

1746 ) 

1747 if forwarded_response is not None: 

1748 # Request was handled by another worker - convert response to expected format 

1749 if "error" in forwarded_response: 

1750 raise Exception(forwarded_response["error"].get("message", "Forwarded request failed")) # pylint: disable=broad-exception-raised 

1751 result_data = forwarded_response.get("result", {}) 

1752 

1753 def _rehydrate_content_items(items: Any) -> list[types.TextContent | types.ImageContent | types.AudioContent | types.ResourceLink | types.EmbeddedResource]: 

1754 """Convert forwarded tool result items back to MCP content types. 

1755 

1756 Args: 

1757 items: List of content item dicts from forwarded response. 

1758 

1759 Returns: 

1760 List of validated MCP content type instances. 

1761 """ 

1762 if not isinstance(items, list): 

1763 return [] 

1764 converted: list[types.TextContent | types.ImageContent | types.AudioContent | types.ResourceLink | types.EmbeddedResource] = [] 

1765 for item in items: 

1766 if not isinstance(item, dict): 

1767 continue 

1768 item_type = item.get("type") 

1769 try: 

1770 if item_type == "text": 

1771 converted.append(types.TextContent.model_validate(item)) 

1772 elif item_type == "image": 

1773 converted.append(types.ImageContent.model_validate(item)) 

1774 elif item_type == "audio": 

1775 converted.append(types.AudioContent.model_validate(item)) 

1776 elif item_type == "resource_link": 

1777 converted.append(types.ResourceLink.model_validate(item)) 

1778 elif item_type == "resource": 

1779 converted.append(types.EmbeddedResource.model_validate(item)) 

1780 else: 

1781 converted.append(types.TextContent(type="text", text=item if isinstance(item, str) else orjson.dumps(item).decode())) 

1782 except Exception: 

1783 converted.append(types.TextContent(type="text", text=item if isinstance(item, str) else orjson.dumps(item).decode())) 

1784 return converted 

1785 

1786 unstructured = _rehydrate_content_items(result_data.get("content", [])) 

1787 structured = result_data.get("structuredContent") or result_data.get("structured_content") 

1788 if not isinstance(structured, dict): 

1789 structured = None 

1790 is_error = bool(result_data.get("isError") or result_data.get("is_error")) 

1791 if is_error: 

1792 # Preserve the upstream error payload verbatim (#4202). Wrap 

1793 # in CallToolResult so the MCP SDK's server-side 

1794 # ``isinstance(results, types.CallToolResult)`` short-circuit 

1795 # (in ``mcp.server.lowlevel.server``) skips re-validation 

1796 # and doesn't clobber the message with "Output validation 

1797 # error: outputSchema defined but no structured output 

1798 # returned". 

1799 return types.CallToolResult( 

1800 content=unstructured, 

1801 structuredContent=structured, 

1802 isError=True, 

1803 ) 

1804 # Success path: return the list/tuple shape so the MCP SDK's 

1805 # server-side validator runs and enforces the tool's 

1806 # outputSchema against the structured payload. 

1807 if structured: 

1808 return (unstructured, structured) 

1809 return unstructured 

1810 except RuntimeError: 

1811 # Pool not initialized - execute locally 

1812 pass 

1813 

1814 try: 

1815 async with get_db() as db: 

1816 # Use tool service for all tool invocations (handles direct_proxy internally) 

1817 result = await tool_service.invoke_tool( 

1818 db=db, 

1819 name=name, 

1820 arguments=arguments, 

1821 request_headers=request_headers, 

1822 app_user_email=app_user_email, 

1823 user_email=user_email, 

1824 token_teams=token_teams, 

1825 server_id=server_id, 

1826 meta_data=meta_data, 

1827 ) 

1828 if not result or not result.content: 

1829 logger.warning("No content returned by tool: %s", name) 

1830 return [] 

1831 

1832 # Normalize unstructured content to MCP SDK types, preserving metadata (annotations, _meta, size) 

1833 # Helper to convert gateway Annotations to dict for MCP SDK compatibility 

1834 # (mcpgateway.common.models.Annotations != mcp.types.Annotations) 

1835 def _convert_annotations(ann: Any) -> dict[str, Any] | None: 

1836 """Convert gateway Annotations to dict for MCP SDK compatibility. 

1837 

1838 Args: 

1839 ann: Gateway Annotations object, dict, or None. 

1840 

1841 Returns: 

1842 Dict representation of annotations, or None. 

1843 """ 

1844 if ann is None: 

1845 return None 

1846 if isinstance(ann, dict): 

1847 return ann 

1848 if hasattr(ann, "model_dump"): 

1849 return ann.model_dump(by_alias=True, mode="json") 

1850 return None 

1851 

1852 def _convert_meta(meta: Any) -> dict[str, Any] | None: 

1853 """Convert gateway meta to dict for MCP SDK compatibility. 

1854 

1855 Args: 

1856 meta: Gateway meta object, dict, or None. 

1857 

1858 Returns: 

1859 Dict representation of meta, or None. 

1860 """ 

1861 if meta is None: 

1862 return None 

1863 if isinstance(meta, dict): 

1864 return meta 

1865 if hasattr(meta, "model_dump"): 

1866 return meta.model_dump(by_alias=True, mode="json") 

1867 return None 

1868 

1869 unstructured: list[types.TextContent | types.ImageContent | types.AudioContent | types.ResourceLink | types.EmbeddedResource] = [] 

1870 for content in result.content: 

1871 if content.type == "text": 

1872 unstructured.append( 

1873 types.TextContent( 

1874 type="text", 

1875 text=content.text, 

1876 annotations=_convert_annotations(getattr(content, "annotations", None)), 

1877 _meta=_convert_meta(getattr(content, "meta", None)), 

1878 ) 

1879 ) 

1880 elif content.type == "image": 

1881 unstructured.append( 

1882 types.ImageContent( 

1883 type="image", 

1884 data=content.data, 

1885 mimeType=content.mime_type, 

1886 annotations=_convert_annotations(getattr(content, "annotations", None)), 

1887 _meta=_convert_meta(getattr(content, "meta", None)), 

1888 ) 

1889 ) 

1890 elif content.type == "audio": 

1891 unstructured.append( 

1892 types.AudioContent( 

1893 type="audio", 

1894 data=content.data, 

1895 mimeType=content.mime_type, 

1896 annotations=_convert_annotations(getattr(content, "annotations", None)), 

1897 _meta=_convert_meta(getattr(content, "meta", None)), 

1898 ) 

1899 ) 

1900 elif content.type == "resource_link": 

1901 unstructured.append( 

1902 types.ResourceLink( 

1903 type="resource_link", 

1904 uri=content.uri, 

1905 name=content.name, 

1906 description=getattr(content, "description", None), 

1907 mimeType=getattr(content, "mime_type", None), 

1908 size=getattr(content, "size", None), 

1909 _meta=_convert_meta(getattr(content, "meta", None)), 

1910 ) 

1911 ) 

1912 elif content.type == "resource": 

1913 # EmbeddedResource - pass through the model dump as the MCP SDK type requires complex nested structure 

1914 unstructured.append(types.EmbeddedResource.model_validate(content.model_dump(by_alias=True, mode="json"))) 

1915 else: 

1916 # Unknown content type - convert to text representation 

1917 unstructured.append(types.TextContent(type="text", text=orjson.dumps(content.model_dump(by_alias=True, mode="json")).decode())) 

1918 

1919 # If the tool produced structured content (ToolResult.structured_content / structuredContent), 

1920 # return a combination (unstructured, structured) so the server can validate against outputSchema. 

1921 # The ToolService may populate structured_content (snake_case) or the model may expose 

1922 # an alias 'structuredContent' when dumped via model_dump(by_alias=True). 

1923 structured = None 

1924 try: 

1925 # Prefer attribute if present 

1926 structured = getattr(result, "structured_content", None) 

1927 except Exception: 

1928 structured = None 

1929 

1930 # Fallback to by-alias dump (in case the result is a pydantic model with alias fields) 

1931 if not isinstance(structured, dict): 

1932 try: 

1933 dump = result.model_dump(by_alias=True) if hasattr(result, "model_dump") else {} 

1934 structured = dump.get("structuredContent") if isinstance(dump, dict) else None 

1935 except Exception: 

1936 structured = None 

1937 

1938 # MCP CallToolResult.structuredContent accepts dict or None only; 

1939 # reject anything else (e.g. stray MagicMocks in tests, bad shapes). 

1940 if not isinstance(structured, dict): 

1941 structured = None 

1942 

1943 is_error = _truthy_is_error(result) 

1944 

1945 if is_error: 

1946 # Preserve the upstream error payload verbatim (#4202). Wrap 

1947 # in CallToolResult so the MCP SDK's server-side 

1948 # ``isinstance(results, types.CallToolResult)`` short-circuit 

1949 # (in ``mcp.server.lowlevel.server``) skips re-validation 

1950 # and doesn't clobber the message with "Output validation 

1951 # error: outputSchema defined but no structured output 

1952 # returned". 

1953 return types.CallToolResult( 

1954 content=unstructured, 

1955 structuredContent=structured, 

1956 isError=True, 

1957 ) 

1958 

1959 # Success path: return the list/tuple shape so the MCP SDK's 

1960 # server-side validator runs and enforces the tool's 

1961 # outputSchema against the structured payload. 

1962 if structured: 

1963 return (unstructured, structured) 

1964 return unstructured 

1965 except Exception as e: 

1966 logger.exception("Error calling tool '%s': %s", name, e) 

1967 # Re-raise the exception so the MCP SDK can properly convert it to an error response 

1968 # This ensures error details are propagated to the client instead of returning empty results 

1969 raise 

1970 

1971 

1972async def _get_request_context_or_default() -> Tuple[str, dict[str, Any], dict[str, Any]]: 

1973 """Retrieves request context information for the current execution. 

1974 

1975 This function resolves request context using the following precedence: 

1976 

1977 1. Context variables (fast path). Used when the handler executes in the 

1978 same async context as the middleware (for example, direct ASGI dispatch). 

1979 2. ASGI scope. The middleware stores resolved context on 

1980 ``scope[_MCPGATEWAY_CONTEXT_KEY]`` before handing off to the MCP SDK. 

1981 Because the SDK passes the same ``scope`` dictionary through to
