1982 ``mcp_app.request_context.request``, this survives task-group 

1983 boundaries where ContextVars may be lost. 

1984 3. Re-authentication fallback. Re-extracts identity from the request's 

1985 Authorization header or cookies. This is the most expensive path and 

1986 may produce a different context shape for anonymous callers (an empty 

1987 dictionary instead of the middleware's canonical 

1988 ``{"is_authenticated": False, ...}`` structure). 

1989 

1990 Returns: 

1991 Tuple[str, dict[str, Any], dict[str, Any]]: A tuple containing: 

1992 

1993 - server_id: The resolved server identifier. 

1994 - request_headers: The request headers as a dictionary. 

1995 - user_context: The resolved user context dictionary. 

1996 """ 

1997 # 1. Try context vars first (fast path) 

1998 s_id = server_id_var.get() 

1999 

2000 # Check if context vars are populated with real data (not defaults) 

2001 if s_id != "default_server_id": 

2002 return s_id, request_headers_var.get(), user_context_var.get() 

2003 

2004 # 2. Try ASGI scope context injected by handle_streamable_http() 

2005 ctx = None 

2006 try: 

2007 ctx = mcp_app.request_context 

2008 request = ctx.request 

2009 if request: 

2010 gw_ctx = getattr(request, "scope", {}).get(_MCPGATEWAY_CONTEXT_KEY) 

2011 if isinstance(gw_ctx, dict): 

2012 return ( 

2013 gw_ctx.get("server_id") or s_id, 

2014 gw_ctx.get("request_headers", {}), 

2015 gw_ctx.get("user_context", {}), 

2016 ) 

2017 except LookupError: 

2018 # Not in a request context — fall through to ContextVar defaults 

2019 return s_id, request_headers_var.get(), user_context_var.get() 

2020 except Exception as e: 

2021 logger.debug("Failed to read %s from scope: %s", _MCPGATEWAY_CONTEXT_KEY, e) 

2022 

2023 # 3. Re-authentication fallback (stateful session path) 

2024 try: 

2025 # Reuse ctx from the scope-reading block above (step 2) to avoid 

2026 # a redundant mcp_app.request_context lookup. 

2027 if ctx is None: 

2028 ctx = mcp_app.request_context 

2029 request = ctx.request 

2030 if not request: 

2031 logger.warning("No request object found in MCP context") 

2032 return s_id, request_headers_var.get(), user_context_var.get() 

2033 

2034 # Extract server_id from URL 

2035 path = request.url.path 

2036 match = _SERVER_ID_RE.search(path) 

2037 if match: 

2038 s_id = match.group("server_id") 

2039 

2040 # Extract headers 

2041 req_headers = dict(request.headers) 

2042 

2043 # Extract and verify user context 

2044 # Use require_auth_header_first to match streamable_http_auth token precedence: 

2045 # configured auth header (default Authorization) > request cookies > jwt_token parameter. 

2046 # When AUTH_HEADER_NAME is customized, the gateway token is read from that 

2047 # header so a downstream-bound Authorization header cannot be misvalidated 

2048 # as the gateway token. 

2049 auth_header = get_auth_header_value(req_headers) 

2050 cookie_token = request.cookies.get("jwt_token") 

2051 

2052 try: 

2053 raw_payload = await require_auth_header_first(auth_header=auth_header, jwt_token=cookie_token, request=request) 

2054 if isinstance(raw_payload, str): # "anonymous" 

2055 user_ctx = {} 

2056 elif isinstance(raw_payload, dict): 

2057 # Normalize raw JWT payload to canonical user context shape 

2058 # (matches streamable_http_auth normalization at lines 2155-2259) 

2059 user_ctx = await _normalize_jwt_payload(raw_payload) 

2060 else: 

2061 user_ctx = {} 

2062 except Exception as e: 

2063 logger.warning("Failed to recover user context in stateful session: %s", e) 

2064 user_ctx = {} 

2065 

2066 return s_id, req_headers, user_ctx 

2067 

2068 except LookupError: 

2069 # Not in a request context 

2070 return s_id, request_headers_var.get(), user_context_var.get() 

2071 except Exception as e: 

2072 logger.exception("Error recovering context in stateful session: %s", e) 

2073 return s_id, request_headers_var.get(), user_context_var.get() 

2074 

2075 

2076async def _normalize_jwt_payload(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]: 

2077 """Normalize a raw JWT payload to the canonical user context shape. 

2078 

2079 Converts raw JWT fields (sub, token_use, nested user.is_admin) into the 

2080 canonical ``{email, teams, is_admin, is_authenticated, token_use}`` dict that MCP 

2081 handlers expect. This mirrors the normalization performed by 

2082 ``streamable_http_auth`` so that the stateful-session fallback path in 

2083 ``_get_request_context_or_default`` returns an identical shape. 

2084 

2085 Args: 

2086 payload: Raw JWT payload dict from ``require_auth_header_first``. 

2087 

2088 Returns: 

2089 Canonical user context dict with keys email, teams, is_admin, is_authenticated, token_use. 

2090 """ 

2091 email = payload.get("sub") or payload.get("email") 

2092 jwt_is_admin = payload.get("is_admin", False) 

2093 if not jwt_is_admin: 

2094 user_info = payload.get("user", {}) 

2095 jwt_is_admin = user_info.get("is_admin", False) if isinstance(user_info, dict) else False 

2096 

2097 # SECURITY: Check for effective admin status (DB is_admin OR RBAC platform_admin). 

2098 # This ensures SSO-provisioned platform_admins get admin bypass on the fallback 

2099 # stateful-session path, matching the primary _auth_jwt path behavior (issue #4070). 

2100 db_user_is_admin = False 

2101 if email: 

2102 # First-Party 

2103 from mcpgateway.utils.admin_check import is_user_admin # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

2104 

2105 with SessionLocal() as db: 

2106 db_user_is_admin = is_user_admin(db, email) 

2107 

2108 effective_is_admin = db_user_is_admin or jwt_is_admin 

2109 

2110 token_use = payload.get("token_use") 

2111 if token_use == "session": # nosec B105 - Not a password; token_use is a JWT claim type 

2112 # Session token: resolve teams from DB/cache via single policy point 

2113 # First-Party 

2114 from mcpgateway.auth import resolve_session_teams # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

2115 

2116 final_teams = await resolve_session_teams(payload, email, {"is_admin": effective_is_admin}) 

2117 else: 

2118 # API token or legacy: use embedded teams from JWT 

2119 # First-Party 

2120 from mcpgateway.auth import normalize_token_teams # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

2121 

2122 final_teams = normalize_token_teams(payload) 

2123 

2124 user_ctx: dict[str, Any] = { 

2125 "email": email, 

2126 "teams": final_teams, 

2127 "is_admin": effective_is_admin, 

2128 "is_authenticated": True, 

2129 "token_use": token_use, 

2130 } 

2131 # Extract scoped permissions from JWT for per-method enforcement 

2132 scopes = payload.get("scopes") or {} 

2133 scoped_perms = scopes.get("permissions") or [] if isinstance(scopes, dict) else [] 

2134 if scoped_perms: 

2135 user_ctx["scoped_permissions"] = scoped_perms 

2136 return user_ctx 

2137 

2138 

2139@mcp_app.list_tools() 

2140async def list_tools() -> List[types.Tool]: 

2141 """ 

2142 Lists all tools available to the MCP Server. 

2143 

2144 Supports two modes based on gateway's gateway_mode: 

2145 - 'cache': Returns tools from database (default behavior) 

2146 - 'direct_proxy': Proxies the request directly to the remote MCP server 

2147 

2148 Returns: 

2149 A list of Tool objects containing metadata such as name, description, and input schema. 

2150 Logs and returns an empty list on failure. 

2151 

2152 Raises: 

2153 PermissionError: If the caller lacks ``tools.read`` permission. 

2154 

2155 Examples: 

2156 >>> # Test list_tools function signature 

2157 >>> import inspect 

2158 >>> sig = inspect.signature(list_tools) 

2159 >>> list(sig.parameters.keys()) 

2160 [] 

2161 >>> sig.return_annotation 

2162 typing.List[mcp.types.Tool] 

2163 """ 

2164 server_id, request_headers, user_context = await _get_request_context_or_default() 

2165 

2166 # Token scope cap: deny early if scoped permissions exclude tools.read 

2167 if _should_enforce_streamable_rbac(user_context): 

2168 if not _check_scoped_permission(user_context, "tools.read"): 

2169 raise PermissionError(_ACCESS_DENIED_MSG) 

2170 

2171 # First-Party 

2172 from mcpgateway.auth_context import get_scoped_visibility_from_user_context # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

2173 

2174 # Extract Layer-1 visibility filter from user context 

2175 user_email, token_teams = get_scoped_visibility_from_user_context(user_context) 

2176 

2177 # Enforce per-server OAuth requirement in permissive mode (defense-in-depth). 

2178 # When mcp_require_auth=True, the middleware already guarantees authentication. 

2179 # Note: OAuthEnforcementUnavailableError is intentionally uncaught here — 

2180 # the middleware (streamable_http_auth) catches it and returns 503. If the 

2181 # middleware is somehow bypassed, an uncaught 500 is acceptable and will be 

2182 # logged by the ASGI server. 

2183 if not settings.mcp_require_auth: 

2184 await _check_server_oauth_enforcement(server_id, user_context) 

2185 

2186 if server_id: 

2187 try: 

2188 async with get_db() as db: 

2189 # Check for X-Context-Forge-Gateway-Id header first - if present, try direct proxy mode 

2190 gateway_id = extract_gateway_id_from_headers(request_headers) 

2191 

2192 # If X-Context-Forge-Gateway-Id is provided, check if that gateway is in direct_proxy mode 

2193 if gateway_id: 

2194 gateway = db.execute(select(DbGateway).where(DbGateway.id == gateway_id)).scalar_one_or_none() 

2195 if gateway and getattr(gateway, "gateway_mode", "cache") == "direct_proxy" and settings.mcpgateway_direct_proxy_enabled: 

2196 # SECURITY: Check gateway access before allowing direct proxy 

2197 if not await check_gateway_access(db, gateway, user_email, token_teams): 

2198 logger.warning("Access denied to gateway %s in direct_proxy mode for user %s", gateway_id, user_email) 

2199 return [] # Return empty list for unauthorized access 

2200 

2201 # Direct proxy mode: forward request to remote MCP server 

2202 # Get _meta from request context if available 

2203 meta = None 

2204 try: 

2205 request_ctx = mcp_app.request_context 

2206 meta = request_ctx.meta 

2207 logger.info( 

2208 "[LIST TOOLS] Using direct_proxy mode for server %s, gateway %s (from %s header). Meta Attached: %s", 

2209 server_id, 

2210 gateway.id, 

2211 GATEWAY_ID_HEADER, 

2212 meta is not None, 

2213 ) 

2214 except (LookupError, AttributeError) as e: 

2215 logger.debug("No request context available for _meta extraction: %s", e) 

2216 

2217 return await _proxy_list_tools_to_gateway(gateway, request_headers, user_context, meta) 

2218 if gateway: 

2219 logger.debug("Gateway %s found but not in direct_proxy mode (mode: %s), using cache mode", gateway_id, getattr(gateway, "gateway_mode", "cache")) 

2220 else: 

2221 logger.warning("Gateway %s specified in %s header not found", gateway_id, GATEWAY_ID_HEADER) 

2222 

2223 # Check if server exists for cache mode 

2224 server = db.execute(select(DbServer).where(DbServer.id == server_id)).scalar_one_or_none() 

2225 if not server: 

2226 logger.warning("Server %s not found in database", server_id) 

2227 return [] 

2228 

2229 # Default cache mode: use database 

2230 tools = await tool_service.list_server_tools(db, server_id, user_email=user_email, token_teams=token_teams, _request_headers=request_headers) 

2231 return [ 

2232 types.Tool( 

2233 name=tool.name, 

2234 title=_safe_str_attr(tool, "title"), 

2235 description=tool.description or "", 

2236 inputSchema=tool.input_schema, 

2237 outputSchema=tool.output_schema, 

2238 annotations=tool.annotations, 

2239 ) 

2240 for tool in tools 

2241 ] 

2242 except Exception as e: 

2243 logger.error("Error listing tools:%s", e) 

2244 return [] 

2245 else: 

2246 try: 

2247 async with get_db() as db: 

2248 tools, _ = await tool_service.list_tools(db, include_inactive=False, limit=0, user_email=user_email, token_teams=token_teams, _request_headers=request_headers) 

2249 return [ 

2250 types.Tool( 

2251 name=tool.name, 

2252 title=_safe_str_attr(tool, "title"), 

2253 description=tool.description or "", 

2254 inputSchema=tool.input_schema, 

2255 outputSchema=tool.output_schema, 

2256 annotations=tool.annotations, 

2257 ) 

2258 for tool in tools 

2259 ] 

2260 except Exception as e: 

2261 logger.exception("Error listing tools:%s", e) 

2262 return [] 

2263 

2264 

2265@mcp_app.list_prompts() 

2266async def list_prompts() -> List[types.Prompt]: 

2267 """ 

2268 Lists all prompts available to the MCP Server. 

2269 

2270 Returns: 

2271 A list of Prompt objects containing metadata such as name, description, and arguments. 

2272 Logs and returns an empty list on failure. 

2273 

2274 Raises: 

2275 PermissionError: If the user context indicates insufficient permissions (e.g., missing "prompts.read" scope). 

2276 

2277 Examples: 

2278 >>> import inspect 

2279 >>> sig = inspect.signature(list_prompts) 

2280 >>> list(sig.parameters.keys()) 

2281 [] 

2282 >>> sig.return_annotation 

2283 typing.List[mcp.types.Prompt] 

2284 """ 

2285 server_id, _, user_context = await _get_request_context_or_default() 

2286 

2287 # Token scope cap: deny early if scoped permissions exclude prompts.read 

2288 if _should_enforce_streamable_rbac(user_context): 

2289 if not _check_scoped_permission(user_context, "prompts.read"): 

2290 raise PermissionError(_ACCESS_DENIED_MSG) 

2291 

2292 # First-Party 

2293 from mcpgateway.auth_context import get_scoped_visibility_from_user_context # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

2294 

2295 # Extract Layer-1 visibility filter from user context 

2296 user_email, token_teams = get_scoped_visibility_from_user_context(user_context) 

2297 

2298 # Enforce per-server OAuth requirement in permissive mode (defense-in-depth). 

2299 # When mcp_require_auth=True, the middleware already guarantees authentication. 

2300 # Note: OAuthEnforcementUnavailableError is intentionally uncaught here — 

2301 # the middleware (streamable_http_auth) catches it and returns 503. If the 

2302 # middleware is somehow bypassed, an uncaught 500 is acceptable and will be 

2303 # logged by the ASGI server. 

2304 if not settings.mcp_require_auth: 

2305 await _check_server_oauth_enforcement(server_id, user_context) 

2306 

2307 if server_id: 

2308 try: 

2309 async with get_db() as db: 

2310 prompts = await prompt_service.list_server_prompts(db, server_id, user_email=user_email, token_teams=token_teams) 

2311 return [_to_mcp_prompt(prompt) for prompt in prompts] 

2312 except Exception as e: 

2313 logger.exception("Error listing Prompts:%s", e) 

2314 return [] 

2315 else: 

2316 try: 

2317 async with get_db() as db: 

2318 prompts, _ = await prompt_service.list_prompts(db, include_inactive=False, limit=0, user_email=user_email, token_teams=token_teams) 

2319 return [_to_mcp_prompt(prompt) for prompt in prompts] 

2320 except Exception as e: 

2321 logger.exception("Error listing prompts:%s", e) 

2322 return [] 

2323 

2324 

2325@mcp_app.get_prompt() 

2326async def get_prompt(prompt_id: str, arguments: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> types.GetPromptResult: 

2327 """ 

2328 Retrieves a prompt by ID, optionally substituting arguments. 

2329 

2330 Args: 

2331 prompt_id (str): The ID of the prompt to retrieve. 

2332 arguments (Optional[dict[str, str]]): Optional dictionary of arguments to substitute into the prompt. 

2333 

2334 Returns: 

2335 GetPromptResult: Object containing the prompt messages and description. 

2336 Returns an empty list on failure or if no prompt content is found. 

2337 

2338 Raises: 

2339 PermissionError: If the user context indicates insufficient permissions (e.g., missing "prompts.read" scope). 

2340 

2341 Logs exceptions if any errors occur during retrieval. 

2342 

2343 Examples: 

2344 >>> import inspect 

2345 >>> sig = inspect.signature(get_prompt) 

2346 >>> list(sig.parameters.keys()) 

2347 ['prompt_id', 'arguments'] 

2348 >>> sig.return_annotation.__name__ 

2349 'GetPromptResult' 

2350 """ 

2351 server_id, _, user_context = await _get_request_context_or_default() 

2352 

2353 # Token scope cap: deny early if scoped permissions exclude prompts.read 

2354 if _should_enforce_streamable_rbac(user_context): 

2355 if not _check_scoped_permission(user_context, "prompts.read"): 

2356 raise PermissionError(_ACCESS_DENIED_MSG) 

2357 

2358 # First-Party 

2359 from mcpgateway.auth_context import get_scoped_visibility_from_user_context # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

2360 

2361 # Extract Layer-1 visibility filter from user context 

2362 user_email, token_teams = get_scoped_visibility_from_user_context(user_context) 

2363 

2364 # Enforce per-server OAuth requirement in permissive mode (defense-in-depth). 

2365 # When mcp_require_auth=True, the middleware already guarantees authentication. 

2366 # Note: OAuthEnforcementUnavailableError is intentionally uncaught here — 

2367 # the middleware (streamable_http_auth) catches it and returns 503. If the 

2368 # middleware is somehow bypassed, an uncaught 500 is acceptable and will be 

2369 # logged by the ASGI server. 

2370 if not settings.mcp_require_auth: 

2371 await _check_server_oauth_enforcement(server_id, user_context) 

2372 

2373 meta_data = None 

2374 # Extract _meta from request context if available 

2375 try: 

2376 ctx = mcp_app.request_context 

2377 if ctx and ctx.meta is not None: 

2378 meta_data = ctx.meta.model_dump() 

2379 except LookupError: 

2380 # request_context might not be active in some edge cases (e.g. tests) 

2381 logger.debug("No active request context found") 

2382 

2383 try: 

2384 async with get_db() as db: 

2385 try: 

2386 result = await prompt_service.get_prompt( 

2387 db=db, 

2388 prompt_id=prompt_id, 

2389 arguments=arguments, 

2390 user=user_email, 

2391 server_id=server_id, 

2392 token_teams=token_teams, 

2393 _meta_data=meta_data, 

2394 ) 

2395 except Exception as e: 

2396 logger.exception("Error getting prompt '%s': %s", prompt_id, e) 

2397 return [] 

2398 if not result or not result.messages: 

2399 logger.warning("No content returned by prompt: %s", prompt_id) 

2400 return [] 

2401 message_dicts = [message.model_dump() for message in result.messages] 

2402 return types.GetPromptResult(messages=message_dicts, description=result.description) 

2403 except Exception as e: 

2404 logger.exception("Error getting prompt '%s': %s", prompt_id, e) 

2405 return [] 

2406 

2407 

2408@mcp_app.list_resources() 

2409async def list_resources() -> List[types.Resource]: 

2410 """ 

2411 Lists all resources available to the MCP Server. 

2412 

2413 Returns: 

2414 A list of Resource objects containing metadata such as uri, name, description, and mimeType. 

2415 Logs and returns an empty list on failure. 

2416 

2417 Raises: 

2418 PermissionError: If the user context indicates insufficient permissions (e.g., missing "resources.read" scope). 

2419 

2420 Examples: 

2421 >>> import inspect 

2422 >>> sig = inspect.signature(list_resources) 

2423 >>> list(sig.parameters.keys()) 

2424 [] 

2425 >>> sig.return_annotation 

2426 typing.List[mcp.types.Resource] 

2427 """ 

2428 server_id, request_headers, user_context = await _get_request_context_or_default() 

2429 

2430 # Token scope cap: deny early if scoped permissions exclude resources.read 

2431 if _should_enforce_streamable_rbac(user_context): 

2432 if not _check_scoped_permission(user_context, "resources.read"): 

2433 raise PermissionError(_ACCESS_DENIED_MSG) 

2434 

2435 # First-Party 

2436 from mcpgateway.auth_context import get_scoped_visibility_from_user_context # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

2437 

2438 # Extract Layer-1 visibility filter from user context 

2439 user_email, token_teams = get_scoped_visibility_from_user_context(user_context) 

2440 

2441 # Enforce per-server OAuth requirement in permissive mode (defense-in-depth). 

2442 # When mcp_require_auth=True, the middleware already guarantees authentication. 

2443 # Note: OAuthEnforcementUnavailableError is intentionally uncaught here — 

2444 # the middleware (streamable_http_auth) catches it and returns 503. If the 

2445 # middleware is somehow bypassed, an uncaught 500 is acceptable and will be 

2446 # logged by the ASGI server. 

2447 if not settings.mcp_require_auth: 

2448 await _check_server_oauth_enforcement(server_id, user_context) 

2449 

2450 if server_id: 

2451 try: 

2452 async with get_db() as db: 

2453 # Check for X-Context-Forge-Gateway-Id header first for direct proxy mode 

2454 gateway_id = extract_gateway_id_from_headers(request_headers) 

2455 

2456 # If X-Context-Forge-Gateway-Id is provided, check if that gateway is in direct_proxy mode 

2457 if gateway_id: 

2458 gateway = db.execute(select(DbGateway).where(DbGateway.id == gateway_id)).scalar_one_or_none() 

2459 if gateway and gateway.gateway_mode == "direct_proxy" and settings.mcpgateway_direct_proxy_enabled: 

2460 # SECURITY: Check gateway access before allowing direct proxy 

2461 if not await check_gateway_access(db, gateway, user_email, token_teams): 

2462 logger.warning("Access denied to gateway %s in direct_proxy mode for user %s", gateway_id, user_email) 

2463 return [] # Return empty list for unauthorized access 

2464 

2465 # Direct proxy mode: forward request to remote MCP server 

2466 # Get _meta from request context if available 

2467 meta = None 

2468 try: 

2469 request_ctx = mcp_app.request_context 

2470 meta = request_ctx.meta 

2471 logger.info( 

2472 "[LIST RESOURCES] Using direct_proxy mode for server %s, gateway %s (from %s header). Meta Attached: %s", 

2473 server_id, 

2474 gateway.id, 

2475 GATEWAY_ID_HEADER, 

2476 meta is not None, 

2477 ) 

2478 except (LookupError, AttributeError) as e: 

2479 logger.debug("No request context available for _meta extraction: %s", e) 

2480 

2481 return await _proxy_list_resources_to_gateway(gateway, request_headers, user_context, meta) 

2482 if gateway: 

2483 logger.debug("Gateway %s found but not in direct_proxy mode (mode: %s), using cache mode", gateway_id, gateway.gateway_mode) 

2484 else: 

2485 logger.warning("Gateway %s specified in %s header not found", gateway_id, GATEWAY_ID_HEADER) 

2486 

2487 # Default cache mode: use database 

2488 resources = await resource_service.list_server_resources(db, server_id, user_email=user_email, token_teams=token_teams) 

2489 return [ 

2490 types.Resource(uri=resource.uri, name=resource.name, title=_safe_str_attr(resource, "title"), description=resource.description, mimeType=resource.mime_type) 

2491 for resource in resources 

2492 ] 

2493 except Exception as e: 

2494 logger.exception("Error listing Resources:%s", e) 

2495 return [] 

2496 else: 

2497 try: 

2498 async with get_db() as db: 

2499 resources, _ = await resource_service.list_resources(db, include_inactive=False, limit=0, user_email=user_email, token_teams=token_teams) 

2500 return [ 

2501 types.Resource(uri=resource.uri, name=resource.name, title=_safe_str_attr(resource, "title"), description=resource.description, mimeType=resource.mime_type) 

2502 for resource in resources 

2503 ] 

2504 except Exception as e: 

2505 logger.exception("Error listing resources:%s", e) 

2506 return [] 

2507 

2508 

2509@mcp_app.read_resource() 

2510async def read_resource(resource_uri: str) -> Union[str, bytes]: 

2511 """ 

2512 Reads the content of a resource specified by its URI. 

2513 

2514 Args: 

2515 resource_uri (str): The URI of the resource to read. 

2516 

2517 Returns: 

2518 Union[str, bytes]: The content of the resource as text or binary data. 

2519 Returns empty string on failure or if no content is found. 

2520 

2521 Raises: 

2522 PermissionError: If the user does not have the required permissions to read resources. 

2523 

2524 Logs exceptions if any errors occur during reading. 

2525 

2526 Examples: 

2527 >>> import inspect 

2528 >>> sig = inspect.signature(read_resource) 

2529 >>> list(sig.parameters.keys()) 

2530 ['resource_uri'] 

2531 >>> sig.return_annotation 

2532 typing.Union[str, bytes] 

2533 """ 

2534 server_id, request_headers, user_context = await _get_request_context_or_default() 

2535 

2536 # Token scope cap: deny early if scoped permissions exclude resources.read 

2537 if _should_enforce_streamable_rbac(user_context): 

2538 if not _check_scoped_permission(user_context, "resources.read"): 

2539 raise PermissionError(_ACCESS_DENIED_MSG) 

2540 

2541 # First-Party 

2542 from mcpgateway.auth_context import get_scoped_visibility_from_user_context # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

2543 

2544 # Extract Layer-1 visibility filter from user context 

2545 user_email, token_teams = get_scoped_visibility_from_user_context(user_context) 

2546 

2547 # Enforce per-server OAuth requirement in permissive mode (defense-in-depth). 

2548 # When mcp_require_auth=True, the middleware already guarantees authentication. 

2549 # Note: OAuthEnforcementUnavailableError is intentionally uncaught here — 

2550 # the middleware (streamable_http_auth) catches it and returns 503. If the 

2551 # middleware is somehow bypassed, an uncaught 500 is acceptable and will be 

2552 # logged by the ASGI server. 

2553 if not settings.mcp_require_auth: 

2554 await _check_server_oauth_enforcement(server_id, user_context) 

2555 

2556 meta_data = None 

2557 # Extract _meta from request context if available 

2558 try: 

2559 ctx = mcp_app.request_context 

2560 if ctx and ctx.meta is not None: 

2561 meta_data = ctx.meta.model_dump() 

2562 except LookupError: 

2563 # request_context might not be active in some edge cases (e.g. tests) 

2564 logger.debug("No active request context found") 

2565 

2566 try: 

2567 async with get_db() as db: 

2568 # Check for X-Context-Forge-Gateway-Id header first for direct proxy mode 

2569 gateway_id = extract_gateway_id_from_headers(request_headers) 

2570 

2571 # If X-Context-Forge-Gateway-Id is provided, check if that gateway is in direct_proxy mode 

2572 if gateway_id: 

2573 gateway = db.execute(select(DbGateway).where(DbGateway.id == gateway_id)).scalar_one_or_none() 

2574 if gateway and gateway.gateway_mode == "direct_proxy" and settings.mcpgateway_direct_proxy_enabled: 

2575 # SECURITY: Check gateway access before allowing direct proxy 

2576 if not await check_gateway_access(db, gateway, user_email, token_teams): 

2577 logger.warning("Access denied to gateway %s in direct_proxy mode for user %s", gateway_id, user_email) 

2578 return "" 

2579 

2580 # Direct proxy mode: forward request to remote MCP server 

2581 # SECURITY: CWE-532 protection - Log only meta_data key names, NEVER values 

2582 # Metadata may contain PII, authentication tokens, or sensitive context that 

2583 # MUST NOT be written to logs. This is a critical security control. 

2584 logger.debug( 

2585 "Using direct_proxy mode for resources/read %s, server %s, gateway %s (from %s header), forwarding _meta keys: %s", 

2586 resource_uri, 

2587 server_id, 

2588 gateway.id, 

2589 GATEWAY_ID_HEADER, 

2590 sorted(meta_data.keys()) if meta_data else None, 

2591 ) 

2592 # CWE-400: validate _meta limits before network I/O (bypassed in direct-proxy branch) 

2593 _validate_meta_data(meta_data) 

2594 contents = await _proxy_read_resource_to_gateway(gateway, str(resource_uri), user_context, meta_data) 

2595 if contents: 

2596 # Return first content (text or blob) 

2597 first_content = contents[0] 

2598 if hasattr(first_content, "text"): 

2599 return first_content.text 

2600 if hasattr(first_content, "blob"): 

2601 return first_content.blob 

2602 return "" 

2603 if gateway: 

2604 logger.debug("Gateway %s found but not in direct_proxy mode (mode: %s), using cache mode", gateway_id, gateway.gateway_mode) 

2605 else: 

2606 logger.warning("Gateway %s specified in %s header not found", gateway_id, GATEWAY_ID_HEADER) 

2607 

2608 # Default cache mode: use database 

2609 try: 

2610 result = await resource_service.read_resource( 

2611 db=db, 

2612 resource_uri=str(resource_uri), 

2613 user=user_email, 

2614 server_id=server_id, 

2615 token_teams=token_teams, 

2616 meta_data=meta_data, 

2617 ) 

2618 except Exception as e: 

2619 logger.exception("Error reading resource '%s': %s", resource_uri, e) 

2620 return "" 

2621 

2622 # Return blob content if available (binary resources) 

2623 if result and result.blob: 

2624 return result.blob 

2625 

2626 # Return text content if available (text resources) 

2627 if result and result.text: 

2628 return result.text 

2629 

2630 # No content found 

2631 logger.warning("No content returned by resource: %s", resource_uri) 

2632 return "" 

2633 except Exception as e: 

2634 logger.exception("Error reading resource '%s': %s", resource_uri, e) 

2635 return "" 

2636 

2637 

2638@mcp_app.list_resource_templates() 

2639async def list_resource_templates() -> List[Dict[str, Any]]: 

2640 """ 

2641 Lists all resource templates available to the MCP Server. 

2642 

2643 Returns: 

2644 List[types.ResourceTemplate]: A list of resource templates with their URIs and metadata. 

2645 

2646 Raises: 

2647 PermissionError: If the caller lacks ``resources.read`` permission. 

2648 

2649 Examples: 

2650 >>> import inspect 

2651 >>> sig = inspect.signature(list_resource_templates) 

2652 >>> list(sig.parameters.keys()) 

2653 [] 

2654 >>> sig.return_annotation.__origin__.__name__ 

2655 'list' 

2656 """ 

2657 # Extract filtering parameters from user context (same pattern as list_resources) 

2658 server_id, _, user_context = await _get_request_context_or_default() 

2659 

2660 # Token scope cap: deny early if scoped permissions exclude resources.read 

2661 if _should_enforce_streamable_rbac(user_context): 

2662 if not _check_scoped_permission(user_context, "resources.read"): 

2663 raise PermissionError(_ACCESS_DENIED_MSG) 

2664 

2665 # First-Party 

2666 from mcpgateway.auth_context import get_scoped_visibility_from_user_context # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

2667 

2668 # Extract Layer-1 visibility filter from user context 

2669 user_email, token_teams = get_scoped_visibility_from_user_context(user_context) 

2670 

2671 # Enforce per-server OAuth requirement in permissive mode (defense-in-depth). 

2672 # When mcp_require_auth=True, the middleware already guarantees authentication. 

2673 # Note: OAuthEnforcementUnavailableError is intentionally uncaught here — 

2674 # the middleware (streamable_http_auth) catches it and returns 503. If the 

2675 # middleware is somehow bypassed, an uncaught 500 is acceptable and will be 

2676 # logged by the ASGI server. 

2677 if not settings.mcp_require_auth: 

2678 await _check_server_oauth_enforcement(server_id, user_context) 

2679 

2680 try: 

2681 async with get_db() as db: 

2682 try: 

2683 resource_templates = await resource_service.list_resource_templates( 

2684 db, 

2685 user_email=user_email, 

2686 token_teams=token_teams, 

2687 server_id=server_id, 

2688 ) 

2689 return [template.model_dump(by_alias=True) for template in resource_templates] 

2690 except Exception as e: 

2691 logger.exception("Error listing resource templates: %s", e) 

2692 return [] 

2693 except Exception as e: 

2694 logger.exception("Error listing resource templates: %s", e) 

2695 return [] 

2696 

2697 

2698@mcp_app.set_logging_level() 

2699async def set_logging_level(level: types.LoggingLevel) -> types.EmptyResult: 

2700 """ 

2701 Sets the logging level for the MCP Server. 

2702 

2703 Args: 

2704 level (types.LoggingLevel): The desired logging level (debug, info, notice, warning, error, critical, alert, emergency). 

2705 

2706 Returns: 

2707 types.EmptyResult: An empty result indicating success. 

2708 

2709 Examples: 

2710 >>> import inspect 

2711 >>> sig = inspect.signature(set_logging_level) 

2712 >>> list(sig.parameters.keys()) 

2713 ['level'] 

2714 

2715 Raises: 

2716 PermissionError: If the user does not have permission to set the logging level. 

2717 """ 

2718 server_id, _, user_context = await _get_request_context_or_default() 

2719 

2720 # Enforce per-server OAuth requirement in permissive mode (defense-in-depth). 

2721 # When mcp_require_auth=True, the middleware already guarantees authentication. 

2722 # Note: OAuthEnforcementUnavailableError is intentionally uncaught here — 

2723 # the middleware (streamable_http_auth) catches it and returns 503. If the 

2724 # middleware is somehow bypassed, an uncaught 500 is acceptable and will be 

2725 # logged by the ASGI server. 

2726 if not settings.mcp_require_auth: 

2727 await _check_server_oauth_enforcement(server_id, user_context) 

2728 

2729 if _should_enforce_streamable_rbac(user_context): 

2730 # Layer 1: Token scope cap 

2731 if not _check_scoped_permission(user_context, "admin.system_config"): 

2732 raise PermissionError(_ACCESS_DENIED_MSG) 

2733 # Layer 2: RBAC check 

2734 has_permission = await _check_streamable_permission( 

2735 user_context=user_context, 

2736 permission="admin.system_config", 

2737 check_any_team=_check_any_team_for_server_scoped_rbac(user_context, server_id), 

2738 ) 

2739 if not has_permission: 

2740 raise PermissionError(_ACCESS_DENIED_MSG) 

2741 

2742 try: 

2743 # Convert MCP logging level to our LogLevel enum 

2744 level_map = { 

2745 "debug": LogLevel.DEBUG, 

2746 "info": LogLevel.INFO, 

2747 "notice": LogLevel.INFO, 

2748 "warning": LogLevel.WARNING, 

2749 "error": LogLevel.ERROR, 

2750 "critical": LogLevel.CRITICAL, 

2751 "alert": LogLevel.CRITICAL, 

2752 "emergency": LogLevel.CRITICAL, 

2753 } 

2754 log_level = level_map.get(level.lower(), LogLevel.INFO) 

2755 await logging_service.set_level(log_level) 

2756 return types.EmptyResult() 

2757 except PermissionError: 

2758 raise 

2759 except Exception as e: 

2760 logger.exception("Error setting logging level: %s", e) 

2761 return types.EmptyResult() 

2762 

2763 

2764@mcp_app.completion() 

2765async def complete( 

2766 ref: Union[types.PromptReference, types.ResourceTemplateReference], 

2767 argument: types.CompleteRequest, 

2768 context: Optional[types.CompletionContext] = None, 

2769) -> types.CompleteResult: 

2770 """ 

2771 Provides argument completion suggestions for prompts or resources. 

2772 

2773 Args: 

2774 ref: A reference to a prompt or a resource template. Can be either 

2775 `types.PromptReference` or `types.ResourceTemplateReference`. 

2776 argument: The completion request specifying the input text and 

2777 position for which completion suggestions should be generated. 

2778 context: Optional contextual information for the completion request, 

2779 such as user, environment, or invocation metadata. 

2780 

2781 Returns: 

2782 types.CompleteResult: A normalized completion result containing 

2783 completion values, metadata (total, hasMore), and any additional 

2784 MCP-compliant completion fields. 

2785 

2786 Raises: 

2787 PermissionError: If the caller lacks ``tools.read`` permission. 

2788 Exception: If completion handling fails internally. The method 

2789 logs the exception and returns an empty completion structure. 

2790 """ 

2791 # Derive caller visibility scope from the current request context. 

2792 server_id, _, user_context = await _get_request_context_or_default() 

2793 

2794 # Token scope cap: deny early if scoped permissions exclude tools.read 

2795 if _should_enforce_streamable_rbac(user_context): 

2796 if not _check_scoped_permission(user_context, "tools.read"): 

2797 raise PermissionError(_ACCESS_DENIED_MSG) 

2798 

2799 # Enforce per-server OAuth requirement in permissive mode (defense-in-depth). 

2800 # When mcp_require_auth=True, the middleware already guarantees authentication. 

2801 # Note: OAuthEnforcementUnavailableError is intentionally uncaught here — 

2802 # the middleware (streamable_http_auth) catches it and returns 503. If the 

2803 # middleware is somehow bypassed, an uncaught 500 is acceptable and will be 

2804 # logged by the ASGI server. 

2805 if not settings.mcp_require_auth: 

2806 await _check_server_oauth_enforcement(server_id, user_context) 

2807 

2808 try: 

2809 # First-Party 

2810 from mcpgateway.auth_context import get_scoped_visibility_from_user_context # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

2811 

2812 # Extract Layer-1 visibility filter from user context 

2813 user_email, token_teams = get_scoped_visibility_from_user_context(user_context) 

2814 

2815 async with get_db() as db: 

2816 params = { 

2817 "ref": ref.model_dump() if hasattr(ref, "model_dump") else ref, 

2818 "argument": argument.model_dump() if hasattr(argument, "model_dump") else argument, 

2819 "context": context.model_dump() if hasattr(context, "model_dump") else context, 

2820 } 

2821 

2822 result = await completion_service.handle_completion( 

2823 db, 

2824 params, 

2825 user_email=user_email, 

2826 token_teams=token_teams, 

2827 ) 

2828 

2829 # ✅ Normalize the result for MCP 

2830 if isinstance(result, dict): 

2831 completion_data = result.get("completion", result) 

2832 return types.Completion(**completion_data) 

2833 

2834 if hasattr(result, "completion"): 

2835 completion_obj = result.completion 

2836 

2837 # If completion itself is a dict 

2838 if isinstance(completion_obj, dict): 

2839 return types.Completion(**completion_obj) 

2840 

2841 # If completion is another CompleteResult (nested) 

2842 if hasattr(completion_obj, "completion"): 

2843 inner_completion = completion_obj.completion.model_dump() if hasattr(completion_obj.completion, "model_dump") else completion_obj.completion 

2844 return types.Completion(**inner_completion) 

2845 

2846 # If completion is already a Completion model 

2847 if isinstance(completion_obj, types.Completion): 

2848 return completion_obj 

2849 

2850 # If it's another Pydantic model (e.g., mcpgateway.models.Completion) 

2851 if hasattr(completion_obj, "model_dump"): 

2852 return types.Completion(**completion_obj.model_dump()) 

2853 

2854 # If result itself is already a types.Completion 

2855 if isinstance(result, types.Completion): 

2856 return result 

2857 

2858 # Fallback: return empty completion 

2859 return types.Completion(values=[], total=0, hasMore=False) 

2860 

2861 except Exception as e: 

2862 logger.exception("Error handling completion: %s", e) 

2863 return types.Completion(values=[], total=0, hasMore=False) 

2864 

2865 

2866# ----------------------------- POST response interception (ADR-052) ---------------------------- 

2867 

2868 

2869@dataclass(frozen=True) 

2870class _BodyPeekResult: 

2871 """Result of buffering a POST body for response or notification interception. 

2872 

2873 ``body`` is ``None`` when the body cannot be replayed safely — either 

2874 the client disconnected before ``more_body`` went False or the ASGI 

2875 transport raised while we were reading. Callers must NOT replay in 

2876 those cases. 

2877 

2878 When ``too_large`` is True, ``body`` holds only the chunks we had 

2879 already accepted before the cap-busting chunk arrived (so its size 

2880 is at most ``mcp_body_peek_max_bytes``, and may be less). The 

2881 cap-busting chunk itself is passed through to the SDK verbatim via 

2882 ``replay_tail`` — we do **not** slice it into ``body`` and a 

2883 remainder, since that would duplicate the chunk's bytes in memory 

2884 and effectively double the peak footprint we were trying to bound. 

2885 With the no-slice design the over-cap path holds at most 

2886 ``cap + chunk`` bytes (the chunk reference uvicorn already 

2887 allocated, plus whatever we'd buffered before it). 

2888 

2889 ``replay_tail_more_body`` records whether further chunks are still 

2890 pending on the original receive after the tail. Interception is 

2891 skipped on the ``too_large`` path because we couldn't fit the whole 

2892 body in memory to inspect it. 

2893 """ 

2894 

2895 body: Optional[bytes] 

2896 intercepted: bool 

2897 disconnected: bool = False 

2898 too_large: bool = False 

2899 replay_tail: bytes = b"" 

2900 replay_tail_more_body: bool = False 

2901 

2902 def __post_init__(self) -> None: 

2903 """Reject construction sites that build a meaningless combination of fields. 

2904 

2905 Enforces the five invariants the dataclass would otherwise leave 

2906 to convention: 

2907 

2908 1. ``body is None`` ⇔ the body cannot be replayed (``disconnected`` 

2909 must be True; ``intercepted`` and ``too_large`` must be False; 

2910 there cannot be a ``replay_tail``). 

2911 2. ``intercepted=True`` ⇒ we matched a held responder, so ``body`` 

2912 must be set and ``too_large`` must be False. 

2913 3. ``too_large=True`` ⇒ ``body`` is set, ``intercepted`` is False 

2914 (we couldn't parse), and the cap-busting chunk is carried in 

2915 ``replay_tail`` (otherwise the SDK would silently lose data). 

2916 4. ``replay_tail_more_body=True`` ⇒ ``replay_tail`` is non-empty. 

2917 """ 

2918 if self.body is None: 

2919 if not self.disconnected: 

2920 raise ValueError("_BodyPeekResult.body=None is only valid when disconnected=True") 

2921 if self.intercepted or self.too_large or self.replay_tail or self.replay_tail_more_body: 

2922 raise ValueError("_BodyPeekResult: disconnected result must not carry intercepted/too_large/replay_tail") 

2923 return 

2924 # body is not None 

2925 if self.intercepted and self.too_large: 

2926 raise ValueError("_BodyPeekResult: intercepted and too_large are mutually exclusive") 

2927 if self.intercepted and (self.replay_tail or self.replay_tail_more_body): 

2928 # An intercepted POST already short-circuited with 202; the 

2929 # dispatcher never wraps the receive with a replay, so any 

2930 # replay_tail would be silently swallowed. 

2931 raise ValueError("_BodyPeekResult: intercepted result must not carry replay_tail") 

2932 if self.too_large and not self.replay_tail: 

2933 raise ValueError("_BodyPeekResult: too_large result must carry the replay_tail (cap-busting chunk)") 

2934 if self.replay_tail_more_body and not self.replay_tail: 

2935 raise ValueError("_BodyPeekResult: replay_tail_more_body=True requires a non-empty replay_tail") 

2936 

2937 

2938async def _drain_request_body(receive: Receive) -> _BodyPeekResult: 

2939 """Drain ASGI request events into a body buffer with a soft peek cap. 

2940 

2941 Cap (``settings.mcp_body_peek_max_bytes``) bounds the memory the 

2942 body-peek path holds while inspecting. When the buffered prefix 

2943 reaches the cap we stop reading, return what we have with 

2944 ``too_large=True``, and let the caller fall through to the SDK via a 

2945 replay-and-defer wrapper. The SDK's normal streaming receive then 

2946 drains the rest of the body — no traffic is dropped, interception is 

2947 just skipped for that one request. This matters for legitimate large 

2948 payloads such as ``sampling/createMessage`` model output, which can 

2949 exceed the cap and would otherwise be lost if we 413'd. 

2950 

2951 ASGI transport-level errors (``anyio.EndOfStream``, 

2952 ``ClosedResourceError``, ``OSError``) are translated to 

2953 ``disconnected=True`` so the body-peek path stays transparent to 

2954 the SDK fallback. Without this translation a transport hiccup 

2955 here would surface as a 500 from the gateway instead of the 

2956 SDK's normal disconnect handling. 

2957 """ 

2958 cap = settings.mcp_body_peek_max_bytes 

2959 body = bytearray() 

2960 try: 

2961 while True: 

2962 message = await receive() 

2963 if message.get("type") != "http.request": 

2964 return _BodyPeekResult(body=None, intercepted=False, disconnected=True) 

2965 chunk = message.get("body", b"") 

2966 chunk_is_final = not message.get("more_body", False) 

2967 if len(body) + len(chunk) > cap: 

2968 # Soft cap: stop peeking, hand the prefix back so the 

2969 # caller can replay-and-defer to the SDK. Don't 413 — 

2970 # legitimate sampling responses can exceed the cap. 

2971 # 

2972 # Pass the cap-busting chunk through verbatim instead 

2973 # of slicing it into ``body`` and a remainder. Slicing 

2974 # would copy the whole chunk into two new bytes objects, 

2975 # duplicating it in memory — the very amplification the 

2976 # cap is meant to prevent. With the no-slice design the 

2977 # peak footprint stays at ``len(body) + chunk`` bytes 

2978 # (the chunk reference uvicorn already allocated, plus 

2979 # whatever we'd buffered before it). 

2980 return _BodyPeekResult( 

2981 body=bytes(body), 

2982 intercepted=False, 

2983 too_large=True, 

2984 replay_tail=chunk, 

2985 replay_tail_more_body=not chunk_is_final, 

2986 ) 

2987 body.extend(chunk) 

2988 if chunk_is_final: 

2989 return _BodyPeekResult(body=bytes(body), intercepted=False) 

2990 except (anyio.EndOfStream, anyio.ClosedResourceError, OSError) as exc: 

2991 logger.debug("body-peek receive() aborted: %s", exc) 

2992 return _BodyPeekResult(body=None, intercepted=False, disconnected=True) 

2993 

2994 

2995async def _maybe_intercept_response_post( 

2996 *, 

2997 receive: Receive, 

2998 mcp_session_id: str, 

2999 notification_service: Any, 

3000) -> _BodyPeekResult: 

3001 """Drain the POST body and try to route it to a held server-initiated request. 

3002 

3003 Args: 

3004 receive: ASGI receive callable for the in-flight POST. 

3005 mcp_session_id: Validated downstream session id. 

3006 notification_service: Live ``NotificationService`` singleton; the 

3007 holder of the per-session pending-request dict. 

3008 

3009 Returns: 

3010 ``_BodyPeekResult``. ``intercepted=True`` when a held responder 

3011 was matched and resolved (caller should respond 202). 

3012 ``disconnected=True`` when the client dropped mid-body (caller 

3013 should NOT replay; the SDK's disconnect handling will fire on 

3014 its own first ``receive()``). 

3015 """ 

3016 drained = await _drain_request_body(receive) 

3017 if drained.disconnected or drained.body is None: 

3018 return drained 

3019 if drained.too_large: 

3020 # Prefix-only body — can't parse for interception. Pass the 

3021 # drained result through so the dispatch falls through to the 

3022 # SDK with replay-and-defer (preserving too_large). 

3023 return drained 

3024 body = drained.body 

3025 try: 

3026 payload = orjson.loads(body) 

3027 except orjson.JSONDecodeError: 

3028 return _BodyPeekResult(body=body, intercepted=False) 

3029 # Single-message JSON-RPC response (the spec allows batches; for v1 we 

3030 # only intercept the single-message shape — batches fall through). 

3031 if not isinstance(payload, dict): 

3032 return _BodyPeekResult(body=body, intercepted=False) 

3033 if "method" in payload or "id" not in payload: 

3034 return _BodyPeekResult(body=body, intercepted=False) 

3035 if "result" not in payload and "error" not in payload: 

3036 return _BodyPeekResult(body=body, intercepted=False) 

3037 request_id = str(payload["id"]) 

3038 handled = await notification_service.complete_request(mcp_session_id, request_id, payload) 

3039 return _BodyPeekResult(body=body, intercepted=handled) 

3040 

3041 

3042async def _maybe_short_circuit_notification(receive: Receive) -> _BodyPeekResult: 

3043 """Drain a POST body and detect a JSON-RPC notification (no ``id`` field). 

3044 

3045 Spec: a notification is fire-and-forget — the server MUST NOT return 

3046 a JSON-RPC response body. Per the Streamable HTTP spec the prescribed 

3047 HTTP-level acknowledgment is ``202 Accepted`` with an empty body, 

3048 which is what the caller emits when we return ``intercepted=True``. 

3049 Anything else (a request with an ``id``, a malformed body, or 

3050 anything we can't parse) returns ``intercepted=False`` and the 

3051 caller should replay the body to the SDK for normal handling. 

3052 

3053 Returns ``disconnected=True`` when the client dropped mid-body so the 

3054 caller can avoid feeding the SDK a truncated payload. 

3055 """ 

3056 drained = await _drain_request_body(receive) 

3057 if drained.disconnected or drained.body is None: 

3058 return drained 

3059 if drained.too_large: 

3060 # Prefix-only body — can't parse for short-circuit. Pass the 

3061 # drained result through so the dispatch falls through to the 

3062 # SDK with replay-and-defer (preserving too_large). 

3063 return drained 

3064 body = drained.body 

3065 try: 

3066 payload = orjson.loads(body) 

3067 except orjson.JSONDecodeError: 

3068 return _BodyPeekResult(body=body, intercepted=False) 

3069 # Only short-circuit single-message notifications. A batch or anything 

3070 # without a ``method`` falls through to the SDK. 

3071 if not isinstance(payload, dict): 

3072 return _BodyPeekResult(body=body, intercepted=False) 

3073 if "method" not in payload or "id" in payload: 

3074 return _BodyPeekResult(body=body, intercepted=False) 

3075 return _BodyPeekResult(body=body, intercepted=True) 

3076 

3077 

3078_MCP_KNOWN_REQUEST_METHODS = frozenset( 

3079 { 

3080 "initialize", 

3081 "tools/list", 

3082 "list_tools", 

3083 "list_gateways", 

3084 "list_roots", 

3085 "resources/list", 

3086 "resources/read", 

3087 "resources/subscribe", 

3088 "resources/unsubscribe", 

3089 "resources/templates/list", 

3090 "prompts/list", 

3091 "prompts/get", 

3092 "ping", 

3093 "tools/call", 

3094 "roots/list", 

3095 "notifications/initialized", 

3096 "notifications/cancelled", 

3097 "notifications/message", 

3098 "sampling/createMessage", 

3099 "elicitation/create", 

3100 "completion/complete", 

3101 "logging/setLevel", 

3102 } 

3103) 

3104_MCP_KNOWN_REQUEST_PREFIXES = ("roots/", "notifications/", "sampling/", "elicitation/", "completion/", "logging/") 

3105 

3106 

3107def _is_known_mcp_request_method(method: str) -> bool: 

3108 """Return whether ``method`` is handled by the gateway's MCP JSON-RPC dispatcher.""" 

3109 return method in _MCP_KNOWN_REQUEST_METHODS or method.startswith(_MCP_KNOWN_REQUEST_PREFIXES) 

3110 

3111 

3112def _make_replay_receive( 

3113 body: bytes, 

3114 downstream_receive: Receive, 

3115 *, 

3116 replay_tail: bytes = b"", 

3117 replay_tail_more_body: bool = False, 

3118) -> Receive: 

3119 """Return an ASGI receive that yields ``body`` once, then defers to ``downstream_receive``. 

3120 

3121 Used after we have peeked at a POST body for response interception but 

3122 decided not to short-circuit — the SDK still needs to see the body, so 

3123 we replay it before relinquishing receive duty back to the original 

3124 callable for any subsequent disconnect signals. 

3125 

3126 When the peek truncated at the cap, ``replay_tail`` holds the chunk 

3127 bytes we consumed but did not store in ``body``; the replay yields 

3128 them as a second ASGI message before deferring. ``replay_tail_more_body`` 

3129 indicates whether more chunks still need draining from 

3130 ``downstream_receive`` after the tail. Without this, truncating the 

3131 peek would silently drop the unstored chunk bytes from the SDK's 

3132 view of the body. 

3133 

3134 Args: 

3135 body: Buffered request body (≤ peek cap). 

3136 downstream_receive: The original ASGI receive callable to fall back 

3137 to once the buffered messages have been replayed. 

3138 replay_tail: Chunk bytes consumed from receive but not stored in 

3139 ``body`` (over-cap remainder). Empty when no truncation. 

3140 replay_tail_more_body: ``True`` when ``downstream_receive`` still 

3141 has further chunks to deliver after the tail; the SDK then 

3142 keeps calling ``receive()`` to drain them. 

3143 """ 

3144 pending: list[Dict[str, Any]] = [{"type": "http.request", "body": body, "more_body": bool(replay_tail) or replay_tail_more_body}] 

3145 if replay_tail: 

3146 pending.append({"type": "http.request", "body": replay_tail, "more_body": replay_tail_more_body}) 

3147 queue = iter(pending) 

3148 

3149 async def replay_receive() -> Dict[str, Any]: 

3150 """Yield the buffered prefix (and tail if any), then defer to original receive.""" 

3151 try: 

3152 return next(queue) 

3153 except StopIteration: 

3154 return await downstream_receive() 

3155 

3156 return replay_receive 

3157 

3158 

3159# Lazy-populated on first request to avoid the import cycle at module 

3160# load time; caches the MODULE rather than the function so 

3161# ``monkeypatch.setattr(module, "get_notification_service", ...)`` in 

3162# tests still takes effect (the per-call attribute lookup on the 

3163# cached module sees the patched value). 

3164_notification_service_module: Optional[Any] = None 

3165 

3166 

3167def _get_notification_service() -> Any: 

3168 """Return the current ``notification_service.get_notification_service()`` result. 

3169 

3170 The first call imports the module (resolving the cycle that makes a 

3171 top-level import unsafe); subsequent calls re-read the module 

3172 attribute so test monkeypatching continues to work. 

3173 """ 

3174 global _notification_service_module # pylint: disable=global-statement 

3175 if _notification_service_module is None: 

3176 # First-Party 

3177 import mcpgateway.services.notification_service as ns_module # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

3178 

3179 _notification_service_module = ns_module 

3180 return _notification_service_module.get_notification_service() 

3181 

3182 

3183def _resolve_intercept_target(method: str, mcp_session_id: str) -> Optional[Any]: 

3184 """Return the NotificationService when response interception should run, else None. 

3185 

3186 Response interception applies only to POST requests with a session 

3187 id where the service has at least one pending server-initiated 

3188 request. The service's "not initialized" RuntimeError is treated as 

3189 "no interception" (test bootstrap / early startup); other 

3190 exceptions are intentionally not caught — a narrow catch keeps 

3191 bugs in ``has_pending_request`` from silently disabling interception 

3192 for every in-flight responder. 

3193 

3194 Args: 

3195 method: HTTP method. 

3196 mcp_session_id: ``"not-provided"`` when no Mcp-Session-Id header 

3197 was present, else the validated session id. 

3198 

3199 Returns: 

3200 The ``NotificationService`` instance to pass into 

3201 ``_maybe_intercept_response_post``, or ``None`` to skip 

3202 interception entirely. 

3203 """ 

3204 if method != "POST" or mcp_session_id == "not-provided": 

3205 return None 

3206 try: 

3207 svc = _get_notification_service() 

3208 except RuntimeError: 

3209 return None 

3210 if svc.has_pending_request(mcp_session_id): 

3211 return svc 

3212 return None 

3213 

3214 

3215class _PeekDispatchOutcome(Enum): 

3216 """Outcome of dispatching a body-peek result back into the request loop.""" 

3217 

3218 HANDLED = "handled" 

3219 """Helper sent the 202 response; caller should ``return``.""" 

3220 ABORTED = "aborted" 

3221 """Client disconnected mid-body; caller should ``return`` without further action.""" 

3222 FALLTHROUGH = "fallthrough" 

3223 """Caller should continue with the (possibly replay-wrapped) ``receive`` callable.""" 

3224 

3225 

3226async def _dispatch_peek_outcome( 

3227 peek: "_BodyPeekResult", 

3228 receive: Receive, 

3229 send: Send, 

3230 *, 

3231 accepted_body: bytes, 

3232 log_label: str, 

3233 log_context: str, 

3234) -> tuple[_PeekDispatchOutcome, Receive]: 

3235 """Translate a ``_BodyPeekResult`` into the next action for ``handle_streamable_http``. 

3236 

3237 Both the response-interception and notification-short-circuit paths 

3238 follow the same shape: 

3239 

3240 * ``intercepted`` → emit 202 + return 

3241 * ``disconnected`` → log + return 

3242 * ``too_large`` → log and fall through 

3243 * otherwise → wrap ``receive`` with the replay receive 

3244 

3245 Args: 

3246 peek: Result of the body-peek call. 

3247 receive: Original ASGI receive callable. 

3248 send: ASGI send callable used to emit the 202 response. 

3249 accepted_body: Body bytes for the 202 response (``b"{}"`` for 

3250 response interception, ``b""`` for notification 

3251 short-circuit). 

3252 log_label: Short identifier for the path emitting the log 

3253 (e.g. ``"response interception"`` or 

3254 ``"notification short-circuit"``). 

3255 log_context: Per-request identifier used in log lines (session 

3256 id, path, etc.). 

3257 

3258 Returns: 

3259 ``(outcome, receive)`` — the second value is meaningful only for 

3260 ``FALLTHROUGH`` and is the (possibly replay-wrapped) callable 

3261 the caller should pass to the SDK. 

3262 

3263 Raises: 

3264 RuntimeError: If the peek result violates the invariant that 

3265 ``body`` is non-None on the fall-through path. The 

3266 ``_BodyPeekResult.__post_init__`` validator should make this 

3267 unreachable; the explicit raise replaces the prior 

3268 ``assert`` so the check survives ``python -O``. 

3269 """ 

3270 if peek.intercepted: 

3271 await send({"type": "http.response.start", "status": 202, "headers": [(b"content-type", b"application/json")]}) 

3272 await send({"type": "http.response.body", "body": accepted_body}) 

3273 return _PeekDispatchOutcome.HANDLED, receive 

3274 if peek.disconnected: 

3275 # Client dropped mid-body. Don't feed the SDK a truncated payload — 

3276 # let it observe the disconnect on its own next ``receive()`` call. 

3277 logger.debug("POST %s aborted by client mid-body (%s); not replaying", log_context, log_label) 

3278 return _PeekDispatchOutcome.ABORTED, receive 

3279 if peek.too_large: 

3280 # Body exceeded the peek cap. Skip interception and fall through to 

3281 # the SDK with a replay-and-defer wrapper — valid MCP traffic isn't 

3282 # dropped; only this one request misses interception. 

3283 logger.debug("POST %s body exceeds peek cap during %s; falling through to SDK", log_context, log_label) 

3284 if peek.body is None: # type-narrow for mypy; invariant rules this out 

3285 raise RuntimeError("_BodyPeekResult invariant violation: body=None on fall-through") 

3286 new_receive = _make_replay_receive( 

3287 peek.body, 

3288 receive, 

3289 replay_tail=peek.replay_tail, 

3290 replay_tail_more_body=peek.replay_tail_more_body, 

3291 ) 

3292 return _PeekDispatchOutcome.FALLTHROUGH, new_receive 

3293 

3294 

3295# ----------------------------- GET /mcp stream ----------------------------- 

3296# 

3297# ADR-052: spec-conformant server→client SSE stream. Any node accepts the GET 

3298# (no affinity check); messages are delivered via the per-session event bus 

3299# which fans out across nodes when running on Redis. The single-listener 

3300# invariant is enforced via SessionAffinity.claim_listener. 

3301 

3302# How many heartbeats per TTL — gives us "lose at most TTL/N before 

3303# expiring" margin. 3 means a single missed beat is recoverable on the 

3304# next attempt without losing the claim. Floor of 1s prevents 

3305# pathological busy-looping if an operator sets the TTL very low. 

3306_GET_STREAM_HEARTBEAT_INTERVALS_PER_TTL = 3 

3307_GET_STREAM_HEARTBEAT_MIN_INTERVAL_SECONDS = 1.0 

3308 

3309 

3310def _heartbeat_interval_seconds() -> float: 

3311 """Derive the heartbeat refresh cadence from the configured listener TTL. 

3312 

3313 Returns ``ttl / _GET_STREAM_HEARTBEAT_INTERVALS_PER_TTL``, floored 

3314 at ``_GET_STREAM_HEARTBEAT_MIN_INTERVAL_SECONDS``. Deriving from 

3315 the TTL means an operator-configured TTL value lower than the 

3316 minimum interval still produces a sane cadence; a hard-coded 

3317 cadence would let the claim TTL expire before the next heartbeat 

3318 fired. 

3319 """ 

3320 ttl = float(settings.mcp_get_stream_listener_ttl_seconds) 

3321 return max(_GET_STREAM_HEARTBEAT_MIN_INTERVAL_SECONDS, ttl / _GET_STREAM_HEARTBEAT_INTERVALS_PER_TTL) 

3322 

3323 

3324# How many consecutive heartbeat failures we tolerate before treating 

3325# the claim as lost. N=2 rides out one transient Redis hiccup at 

3326# heartbeat time without tearing down an otherwise-healthy stream. 

3327# Stays well under the TTL: with the default 30s TTL and ~10s heartbeat 

3328# cadence, 2 misses = 20s, comfortably below 30s. 

3329_GET_STREAM_HEARTBEAT_FAILURE_TOLERANCE = 2 

3330 

3331# Metric label used when the JSON-RPC method on the wire isn't a string — 

3332# defensive guard against malformed envelopes that nonetheless deliver. 

3333_UNKNOWN_METHOD_LABEL = "unknown" 

3334 

3335# Allowlist of MCP method labels that the events-delivered counter is 

3336# allowed to emit. Anything outside this set buckets to ``other``. The 

3337# method name comes from upstream JSON-RPC traffic, so an unbucketed 

3338# label would let a buggy or malicious upstream server explode 

3339# Prometheus cardinality and exhaust scrape memory. Keep this list 

3340# narrow — add new entries when a real MCP method graduates from 

3341# ``other`` and you want a dedicated bucket. 

3342_KNOWN_MCP_METHOD_LABELS: frozenset[str] = frozenset( 

3343 { 

3344 "notifications/initialized", 

3345 "notifications/cancelled", 

3346 "notifications/progress", 

3347 "notifications/message", 

3348 "notifications/tools/list_changed", 

3349 "notifications/resources/list_changed", 

3350 "notifications/resources/updated", 

3351 "notifications/prompts/list_changed", 

3352 "notifications/roots/list_changed", 

3353 "sampling/createMessage", 

3354 "elicitation/create", 

3355 "roots/list", 

3356 "logging/setLevel", 

3357 "ping", 

3358 } 

3359) 

3360_OTHER_METHOD_LABEL = "other" 

3361 

3362 

3363def _accepts_event_stream(accept_header: str) -> bool: 

3364 """Return True iff the Accept header allows ``text/event-stream``. 

3365 

3366 Case-insensitive media-type match, honours ``;q=0`` as 

3367 "explicitly not wanted", and treats an empty / missing header as 

3368 permissive (curl-style clients). Per RFC 7231 §5.3.2 — substring 

3369 matching gets both edge cases wrong: ``Text/Event-Stream`` rejected 

3370 and ``Accept: text/event-stream;q=0`` accepted. 

3371 """ 

3372 if not accept_header: 

3373 return True # absence of preference → no restriction 

3374 for entry in accept_header.split(","): 

3375 entry = entry.strip() 

3376 if not entry: 

3377 continue 

3378 media_type, _, params = entry.partition(";") 

3379 media_type = media_type.strip().lower() 

3380 if media_type not in ("text/event-stream", "text/*", "*/*"): 

3381 continue 

3382 # q-value defaults to 1.0; 0 means explicit refusal. 

3383 q_value = 1.0 

3384 for param in params.split(";"): 

3385 name, _, value = param.partition("=") 

3386 if name.strip().lower() == "q": 

3387 try: 

3388 q_value = float(value.strip()) 

3389 except ValueError: 

3390 q_value = 0.0 

3391 break 

3392 if q_value > 0: 

3393 return True 

3394 return False 

3395 

3396 

3397def _bucket_method_label(method: Optional[str]) -> str: 

3398 """Map an MCP method name to a bounded Prometheus label value.""" 

3399 if not isinstance(method, str) or not method: 

3400 return _UNKNOWN_METHOD_LABEL 

3401 if method in _KNOWN_MCP_METHOD_LABELS: 

3402 return method 

3403 return _OTHER_METHOD_LABEL 

3404 

3405 

3406async def _handle_get_stream( 

3407 *, 

3408 scope: Scope, 

3409 receive: Receive, 

3410 send: Send, 

3411 mcp_session_id: str, 

3412 last_event_id: Optional[str], 

3413 accept: str, 

3414) -> None: 

3415 """Serve a GET /mcp SSE stream for ``mcp_session_id``. 

3416 

3417 Negotiates the listener claim, opens an :class:`EventSourceResponse` 

3418 backed by the :class:`ServerEventBus`, and refreshes the claim while 

3419 the connection is held. 

3420 

3421 Args: 

3422 scope: ASGI scope for the inbound GET request. 

3423 receive: ASGI receive callable. 

3424 send: ASGI send callable. 

3425 mcp_session_id: Validated downstream session id from 

3426 ``Mcp-Session-Id``. 

3427 last_event_id: Value of the ``Last-Event-Id`` header, or None. 

3428 accept: Value of the ``Accept`` header (used for content negotiation). 

3429 

3430 Notes: 

3431 Per the MCP spec, GET requires ``Accept: text/event-stream``. We 

3432 return 406 if the client did not accept it; 409 if another 

3433 listener already holds the claim for this session; and 503 in 

3434 any of three cases — ``SessionAffinity`` singleton missing, 

3435 ``ListenerClaimResult.UNAVAILABLE`` (claim-storage backend 

3436 failed), or ``get_server_event_bus()`` raised. All three 503 

3437 paths share the same ``bus_unavailable`` label on 

3438 ``transport_get_rejected_total``; separating them would 

3439 require distinct outcome labels, filed as a potential 

3440 follow-up if operators need to split. 

3441 """ 

3442 # Third-Party 

3443 from sse_starlette.sse import EventSourceResponse # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

3444 

3445 # First-Party 

3446 from mcpgateway.services.session_affinity import ( # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

3447 get_session_affinity, 

3448 ListenerClaimResult, 

3449 SessionAffinityNotInitializedError, 

3450 ) 

3451 from mcpgateway.transports.server_event_bus import ( # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

3452 BusBackendError, 

3453 get_server_event_bus, 

3454 ListenerBacklogOverflow, 

3455 ) 

3456 

3457 if not _accepts_event_stream(accept): 

3458 transport_get_rejected_counter.labels(outcome="not_acceptable").inc() 

3459 await ORJSONResponse( 

3460 {"detail": "GET /mcp requires Accept: text/event-stream"}, 

3461 status_code=406, 

3462 )(scope, receive, send) 

3463 return 

3464 

3465 # Resolve the process-wide SessionAffinity singleton. ADR-052: the 

3466 # listener-claim dict lives on the singleton regardless of whether 

3467 # cross-worker Redis affinity is enabled, so ``main.lifespan`` 

3468 # always initializes it. A transient-fallback singleton would 

3469 # break the single-listener invariant: each request would get a 

3470 # fresh instance with its own dict, so two GETs on the same 

3471 # session both win the claim. 

3472 try: 

3473 affinity = get_session_affinity() 

3474 except SessionAffinityNotInitializedError: 

3475 # Early-boot / test-bootstrap race. Fail closed with 503 — we 

3476 # cannot enforce the invariant without the singleton, and in any 

3477 # real deployment lifespan initializes the service before the 

3478 # HTTP listener accepts traffic. 

3479 logger.warning("GET /mcp: SessionAffinity not initialized; returning 503") 

3480 transport_get_rejected_counter.labels(outcome="bus_unavailable").inc() 

3481 await ORJSONResponse( 

3482 {"detail": "Session affinity service not initialized; retry shortly."}, 

3483 status_code=503, 

3484 headers={"Retry-After": "1"}, 

3485 )(scope, receive, send) 

3486 return 

3487 

3488 connection_id = str(uuid4()) 

3489 claim_result = await affinity.claim_listener(mcp_session_id, connection_id) 

3490 # match + assert_never makes the consumer exhaustive: a future 

3491 # ListenerClaimResult variant added without updating this branch 

3492 # is a type-checker error rather than a silent fall-through. 

3493 match claim_result: 

3494 case ListenerClaimResult.CONFLICT: 

3495 transport_get_rejected_counter.labels(outcome="listener_conflict").inc() 

3496 await ORJSONResponse( 

3497 {"detail": "Another GET /mcp stream is already open for this session."}, 

3498 status_code=409, 

3499 headers={"Retry-After": "1"}, 

3500 )(scope, receive, send) 

3501 return 

3502 case ListenerClaimResult.UNAVAILABLE: 

3503 # Distinct from CONFLICT: storage backing the claim failed 

3504 # (Redis unreachable, configured-but-no-client, eval errored). 

3505 # 503 lets operators distinguish backend outage from real 

3506 # listener races. 

3507 transport_get_rejected_counter.labels(outcome="bus_unavailable").inc() 

3508 await ORJSONResponse( 

3509 {"detail": "Listener-claim storage unavailable; retry shortly."}, 

3510 status_code=503, 

3511 headers={"Retry-After": "1"}, 

3512 )(scope, receive, send) 

3513 return 

3514 case ListenerClaimResult.WON: 

3515 pass # fall through into the SSE stream setup below 

3516 case _ as _unreachable: 

3517 assert_never(_unreachable) 

3518 

3519 # The finally block below runs three independent cleanups: release 

3520 # the listener claim (always safe — release_listener is a no-op for 

3521 # non-owners), decrement the gauge (guarded by 

3522 # ``gauge_incremented`` so we only dec what we inc'd), and log the 

3523 # missing-response case. Each step has its own try/except so one 

3524 # failure doesn't skip the others. 

3525 bus = None 

3526 heartbeat_task: Optional[asyncio.Task[None]] = None 

3527 cancel_stream_event = asyncio.Event() 

3528 response_invoked = False 

3529 gauge_incremented = False 

3530 try: 

3531 transport_get_active_listeners_gauge.inc() 

3532 gauge_incremented = True 

3533 try: 

3534 bus = await get_server_event_bus() 

3535 except (RuntimeError, BusBackendError) as exc: 

3536 # Configured-but-unavailable backends raise these; broader 

3537 # exceptions (programming errors, misconfiguration) propagate 

3538 # to the outer except so the failure has a real traceback in 

3539 # the log instead of being smuggled into a 503. 

3540 logger.warning("GET /mcp: event bus unavailable for session %s: %s", mcp_session_id, exc) 

3541 transport_get_rejected_counter.labels(outcome="bus_unavailable").inc() 

3542 await ORJSONResponse( 

3543 {"detail": "Event bus unavailable; retry shortly."}, 

3544 status_code=503, 

3545 headers={"Retry-After": "1"}, 

3546 )(scope, receive, send) 

3547 return 

3548 

3549 heartbeat_interval = _heartbeat_interval_seconds() 

3550 consecutive_failures = 0 

3551 

3552 async def heartbeat_loop() -> None: 

3553 """Refresh the listener claim periodically; signal the event generator to close on loss. 

3554 

3555 Tolerates ``_GET_STREAM_HEARTBEAT_FAILURE_TOLERANCE`` 

3556 consecutive failures before treating the claim as lost. Without 

3557 this, a single Redis hiccup at heartbeat time would tear down 

3558 the stream even though the Redis-side TTL hadn't actually 

3559 expired — letting an attacker who can perturb Redis disconnect 

3560 active streams on demand. 

3561 """ 

3562 nonlocal consecutive_failures 

3563 while True: 

3564 await asyncio.sleep(heartbeat_interval) 

3565 still_ours = await affinity.heartbeat_listener(mcp_session_id, connection_id) 

3566 if still_ours: 

3567 consecutive_failures = 0 

3568 continue 

3569 consecutive_failures += 1 

3570 if consecutive_failures < _GET_STREAM_HEARTBEAT_FAILURE_TOLERANCE: 

3571 logger.debug( 

3572 "GET /mcp heartbeat for %s missed (%d/%d); will retry", 

3573 mcp_session_id, 

3574 consecutive_failures, 

3575 _GET_STREAM_HEARTBEAT_FAILURE_TOLERANCE, 

3576 ) 

3577 continue 

3578 # Lost the claim (preempted, TTL expired, or sustained 

3579 # heartbeat failure). The single-listener invariant says 

3580 # we must close THIS stream — otherwise a second GET that 

3581 # re-claims would coexist with us. Set the cancel event; 

3582 # event_gen exits and sse_starlette tears down the 

3583 # response. 

3584 logger.info( 

3585 "GET /mcp listener claim for %s lost after %d consecutive heartbeat failures; ending stream", 

3586 mcp_session_id, 

3587 consecutive_failures, 

3588 ) 

3589 cancel_stream_event.set() 

3590 return 

3591 

3592 heartbeat_task = asyncio.create_task(heartbeat_loop(), name=f"get-stream-heartbeat:{mcp_session_id[:8]}") 

3593 

3594 async def event_gen(): 

3595 """Drain the event bus and yield SSE-shaped frames; signaled by ``cancel_stream_event`` on listener-claim loss.""" 

3596 bus_iter = aiter(bus.subscribe(mcp_session_id, last_event_id=last_event_id)) 

3597 try: 

3598 while True: 

3599 next_event_task = asyncio.create_task(anext(bus_iter)) 

3600 cancel_wait_task = asyncio.create_task(cancel_stream_event.wait()) 

3601 try: 

3602 done, _pending = await asyncio.wait( 

3603 {next_event_task, cancel_wait_task}, 

3604 return_when=asyncio.FIRST_COMPLETED, 

3605 ) 

3606 finally: 

3607 if not next_event_task.done(): 

3608 next_event_task.cancel() 

3609 if not cancel_wait_task.done(): 

3610 cancel_wait_task.cancel() 

3611 if cancel_wait_task in done: 

3612 return 

3613 try: 

3614 event = next_event_task.result() 

3615 except StopAsyncIteration: 

3616 return 

3617 root = getattr(event.message, "root", None) 

3618 method_attr = getattr(root, "method", None) if root is not None else None 

3619 transport_get_events_delivered_counter.labels(method=_bucket_method_label(method_attr)).inc() 

3620 yield { 

3621 "id": event.event_id, 

3622 "event": "message", 

3623 "data": event.message.model_dump_json(by_alias=True, exclude_none=True), 

3624 } 

3625 except ListenerBacklogOverflow: 

3626 logger.info( 

3627 "GET /mcp listener for %s dropped: backlog overflow (client should reconnect with Last-Event-Id)", 

3628 mcp_session_id, 

3629 ) 

3630 except BusBackendError as exc: 

3631 logger.warning( 

3632 "GET /mcp listener for %s dropped: bus backend error: %s", 

3633 mcp_session_id, 

3634 exc, 

3635 ) 

3636 except Exception as exc: # noqa: BLE001 — catch-all guards the SSE response; log with traceback (CancelledError is a BaseException and already propagates) 

3637 # The named branches above cover the documented bus 

3638 # failure modes; anything else is a programming error 

3639 # (label-cardinality bug, SDK shape change, etc.) and 

3640 # would silently terminate the stream every time 

3641 # without a traceback. ``exc_info`` preserves the stack 

3642 # so the cause survives in operator logs / Sentry. 

3643 logger.warning( 

3644 "GET /mcp event generator for %s exited unexpectedly: %s", 

3645 mcp_session_id, 

3646 exc, 

3647 exc_info=exc, 

3648 ) 

3649 finally: 

3650 # Close the bus iterator so its own finally block runs 

3651 # immediately — that's what cancels the Pub/Sub pump task 

3652 # and aclose's the redis pubsub. Without this aclose, the 

3653 # bus generator only finalizes on GC, leaking the pubsub 

3654 # connection and pump task in the meantime. 

3655 try: 

3656 await bus_iter.aclose() 

3657 except Exception as exc: # noqa: BLE001 — best-effort, log so leaks are visible 

3658 logger.debug("bus_iter.aclose raised for %s: %s", mcp_session_id, exc) 

3659 

3660 response = EventSourceResponse( 

3661 event_gen(), 

3662 headers={ 

3663 "Cache-Control": "no-cache", 

3664 "Connection": "keep-alive", 

3665 "X-Accel-Buffering": "no", 

3666 }, 

3667 ) 

3668 response_invoked = True 

3669 await response(scope, receive, send) 

3670 finally: 

3671 # Each cleanup step gets its own try/except so a failure in one 

3672 # doesn't skip the others. A single bare ``await 

3673 # release_listener`` would skip the gauge decrement on a Redis 

3674 # hiccup and let the gauge drift upward forever; in Redis mode 

3675 # the orphaned claim would also block subsequent GETs on that 

3676 # session until TTL expiry. 

3677 if heartbeat_task is not None: 

3678 heartbeat_task.cancel() 

3679 try: 

3680 await heartbeat_task 

3681 except asyncio.CancelledError: 

3682 pass 

3683 except Exception as exc: # noqa: BLE001 — log so a regression in heartbeat_loop has a forensic trail 

3684 logger.warning( 

3685 "Heartbeat task drain raised for %s during GET /mcp cleanup: %s", 

3686 mcp_session_id, 

3687 exc, 

3688 exc_info=exc, 

3689 ) 

3690 try: 

3691 await affinity.release_listener(mcp_session_id, connection_id) 

3692 except Exception as exc: # noqa: BLE001 — log, don't skip the gauge dec below 

3693 logger.warning( 

3694 "release_listener raised for %s during GET /mcp cleanup: %s", 

3695 mcp_session_id, 

3696 exc, 

3697 ) 

3698 if gauge_incremented: 

3699 try: 

3700 transport_get_active_listeners_gauge.dec() 

3701 except Exception as exc: # noqa: BLE001 — Prometheus client failures shouldn't propagate 

3702 logger.debug("gauge.dec raised for %s: %s", mcp_session_id, exc) 

3703 if not response_invoked: 

3704 logger.debug( 

3705 "GET /mcp for %s exited before response was constructed", 

3706 mcp_session_id, 

3707 ) 

3708 

3709 

3710class SessionManagerWrapper: 

3711 """ 

3712 Wrapper class for managing the lifecycle of a StreamableHTTPSessionManager instance. 

3713 Provides start, stop, and request handling methods. 

3714 

3715 Examples: 

3716 >>> # Test SessionManagerWrapper initialization 

3717 >>> wrapper = SessionManagerWrapper() 

3718 >>> wrapper 

3719 <mcpgateway.transports.streamablehttp_transport.SessionManagerWrapper object at ...> 

3720 >>> hasattr(wrapper, 'session_manager') 

3721 True 

3722 >>> hasattr(wrapper, 'stack') 

3723 True 

3724 >>> isinstance(wrapper.stack, AsyncExitStack) 

3725 True 

3726 """ 

3727 

3728 def __init__(self) -> None: 

3729 """ 

3730 Initializes the session manager and the exit stack used for managing its lifecycle. 

3731 

3732 Examples: 

3733 >>> # Test initialization 

3734 >>> wrapper = SessionManagerWrapper() 

3735 >>> wrapper.session_manager is not None 

3736 True 

3737 >>> wrapper.stack is not None 

3738 True 

3739 """ 

3740 

3741 if settings.use_stateful_sessions: 

3742 if settings.experimental_rust_mcp_runtime_enabled and settings.experimental_rust_mcp_session_auth_reuse_enabled and settings.experimental_rust_mcp_event_store_enabled: 

3743 event_store = RustEventStore( 

3744 max_events_per_stream=settings.streamable_http_max_events_per_stream, 

3745 ttl=settings.streamable_http_event_ttl, 

3746 ) 

3747 logger.debug("Using RustEventStore for stateful sessions") 

3748 # Use Redis event store for single-worker stateful deployments 

3749 elif settings.cache_type == "redis" and settings.redis_url: 

3750 event_store = RedisEventStore(max_events_per_stream=settings.streamable_http_max_events_per_stream, ttl=settings.streamable_http_event_ttl) 

3751 logger.debug("Using RedisEventStore for stateful sessions (single-worker)") 

3752 else: 

3753 # Fall back to in-memory for single-worker or when Redis not available 

3754 event_store = InMemoryEventStore() 

3755 logger.warning("Using InMemoryEventStore - only works with single worker!") 

3756 stateless = False 

3757 else: 

3758 event_store = None 

3759 stateless = True 

3760 

3761 self.session_manager = StreamableHTTPSessionManager( 

3762 app=mcp_app, 

3763 event_store=event_store, 

3764 json_response=settings.json_response_enabled, 

3765 stateless=stateless, 

3766 ) 

3767 self.stack = AsyncExitStack() 

3768 

3769 async def initialize(self) -> None: 

3770 """ 

3771 Starts the Streamable HTTP session manager context. 

3772 

3773 Examples: 

3774 >>> # Test initialize method exists 

3775 >>> wrapper = SessionManagerWrapper() 

3776 >>> hasattr(wrapper, 'initialize') 

3777 True 

3778 >>> callable(wrapper.initialize) 

3779 True 

3780 """ 

3781 logger.debug("Initializing Streamable HTTP service") 

3782 await self.stack.enter_async_context(self.session_manager.run()) 

3783 

3784 async def shutdown(self) -> None: 

3785 """ 

3786 Gracefully shuts down the Streamable HTTP session manager. 

3787 

3788 Examples: 

3789 >>> # Test shutdown method exists 

3790 >>> wrapper = SessionManagerWrapper() 

3791 >>> hasattr(wrapper, 'shutdown') 

3792 True 

3793 >>> callable(wrapper.shutdown) 

3794 True 

3795 """ 

3796 logger.debug("Stopping Streamable HTTP Session Manager...") 

3797 await self.stack.aclose() 

3798 

3799 @staticmethod 

3800 async def _validate_server_id(match: "re.Match[str] | None", path: str, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> str | None: 

3801 """Validate and resolve the server_id from the request path. 

3802 

3803 Args: 

3804 match: Result of ``_SERVER_ID_RE.search(path)``. 

3805 path: Original request path (``scope["modified_path"]``). 

3806 scope: ASGI scope dict. 

3807 receive: ASGI receive callable. 

3808 send: ASGI send callable. 

3809 

3810 Returns:
